questions.question: At 500 K, equilibrium constant, {tex}{{\text{K}}_{\text{c}}}{/tex}, for the following reaction is 5.2. {tex}\frac{1}{2}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}\left( {\text{g}} \right) + \frac{1}{2}{\text{ }}{{\text{I}}_{\text{2}}}\left( {\text{g}} \right) \rightleftharpoons {/tex} HI (g) What would be the equilibrium constant Kc for the reaction {tex}2HI(g) \rightleftharpoons {H_2}(g) + {I_2}(g){/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The second equation can be obtained by reversing the 1st reaction. 
{tex}{K_1} = [HI]/{[{H_2}]^{1/2}} \times {[12]^{1/2}}{/tex}
squrare the equation
{tex}{K_1} = {[HI]^2}/[{H_2}] \times [{I_2}]{/tex} ......... (1)
{tex}{K_2} = [{H_2}] \times [{I_2}[/[HI]{/tex} ......... (2)
From eq (1) and (2)
{tex}{({K_1})_2} = 1/{K_2}{/tex}
(5.2)2 = 1/K2
K2 = 1/27.04 = 0.0369

questions.question: A vessel at 1000 K contains CO2 with a pressure of 0.5 atm. Some of the CO2 is converted into CO on the addition of graphite. If the total pressure at equilibrium is 0.8 atm, the value of K is ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
CO2 + C {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2CO
t = 0, 0.5, 0
t = t, 0.5-x, 2x
Ptotal = 0.5 - x + 2x = 0.8
i.e. x = 0.3 atm
i.e. {tex}K = \frac{{{{\left( {{P_{co}}} \right)}^2}}}{{{P_{co2}}}} = \frac{{0.6 \times 0.6}}{{0.2}} = 1.8atm{/tex}

questions.question: One of the reaction that takes place in producing steel from iron ore is the reduction of iron (II) oxide by carbon monoxide to give iron metal and CO2. FeO(s) + CO (g) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Fe (s) + CO2 (g) Kp = 0.265 at 1050 K. What are the equilibrium partial pressures of CO and CO2 at 1050 K if the initial partial pressures are: Pco = 1.4 atm and {tex}P_{CO_2}{/tex} = 0.80 atm?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For the given reaction; {tex}\mathop {FeO + CO(g)}\limits_{1.4atm} = \mathop {Fe(s) + C{O_2}(g)}\limits_{0.80atm} {/tex}
{tex}Q_p=\frac{P_{CO_2}}{P_{CO}}=\frac{0.80}{1.4}=\;0.571{/tex}
It is given that Kp = 0.265
Since QP > Kp
The reaction will proceed in the backward reaction.
Therefore, we can say that the pressure of CO will increase while the pressure of CO2 will decrease.
Now, let the increase in pressure of CO = decrease in pressure of CO2 be P.
Then we can write
{tex}{{K_p} = \frac{{{P_{C{O_2}}}}}{{{P_{CO}}}} = \frac{{0.80 - P}}{{1.4 + P}}}{/tex}
{tex}{ \Rightarrow 0.265 = \frac{{0.80 - P}}{{1.4 + P}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.265 (1.4 + P) = 0.80 - P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.371 + 0.265P = 0.80 - P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1.265P = 0.429 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 0.339atm
Therefore, equilibrium partial pressure of CO2 = 0.80 - 0.339= 0.461 atm
Also, equilibrium partial pressure  of CO = 1.4 + 0.339 = 1.739 atm

questions.question: The equilibrium expression, Kc = [CO2] represents the reaction.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}CaC{O_3}(s) \rightleftharpoons CaO(s) + C{O_2}(g){/tex}
For the given reaction, equilibrium constant can be written as:
{tex}{K_c} = \frac{{\left[ {{C_a}O(s)} \right]\left[ {C{O_2}(g)} \right]}}{{\left[ {{C_a}C{o_3}(s)} \right]}}{/tex}, Where [CaO(s)] = 1, [CaCO3(s) = 1
Therefore, Kc = [CO2(g)]

questions.question:
What is the expression for Kc for the reaction?
 {tex}N_2O(g)\;+\;\frac12O_2(g)\:\rightleftharpoons\;2NO(g){/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}{{N}_{2}}O(g)\ +\ \frac{1}{2}{{O}_{2}}(g)\;\rightleftharpoons \ 2NO(g){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2{{N}_{2}}O(g)\ +\ {{O}_{2}}(g)\;\rightleftharpoons \ 4NO(g){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {{K}_{c}}=\ \frac{{{\left[ NO(g) \right]}^{4}}}{{{\left[ {{N}_{2}}O(g)\ \right]}^{2}}\left[ {{O}_{2}}(g) \right]}{/tex}

questions.question:
For the following reactions, equilibrium constants are given : S(s) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} SO2(g); K1 =1052
2S(s) + 3O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2SO3(g); K2 =10129. The equilibrium constant for the reaction, 2SO(s) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2SO3(g) is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathrm{S}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{2}, K_{1}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \mathrm{SO}_{2} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{S}+\mathrm{O}_{2}, K_{1}^{\prime}=\frac{1}{K_{1}}{/tex}
or, {tex}2 \mathrm{SO}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{S}+2 \mathrm{O}_{2}, K_{1}^{\prime \prime}=\left(K_{1}^{\prime}\right)^{2}=\frac{1}{K_{1}^{2}}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 \mathrm{S}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_{3}, K_{2}{/tex} ...(ii)
Now, [(i) + (ii)] gives
{tex}2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{3}, K_{3}{/tex}
The value of equilibrium constant,
{tex}K_{3}=K_{2} \times K_{1}^{\prime \prime}=K_{2} \times \frac{1}{K_{1}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=10^{129} \times \frac{1}{\left(10^{52}\right)^{2}}=10^{129-104}=10^{25}{/tex}

questions.question: In a chemical reaction, A + 2B {tex}\stackrel{K}{\rightleftharpoons} {/tex} 2C + D, the initial concentration of B was 1.5 times of the concentration of A, but the equilibrium concentrations of A and B were found to be equal. The equilibrium constant (K) for the aforesaid chemical reaction is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For the given chemical reaction,
 [x = degree of dissociation]
Given, at equilibrium.
[A] = [B]
a0 - x = 1.5a0 - 2x
x = 0.5a0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} [A] = a0 - x = a0 - 0.5a0 = 0.5a0
[B] = 1.5a0 - 2x = 1.5a0 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5a0 = 0.5a0
[C] - 2x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5a0= a0
[D] = x = 0.5a0
Now, {tex}K=\frac{[C]^{2}[D]}{[A][B]^{2}}{/tex}
Now, substituting the values in above equation, we get
{tex}K=\frac{\left(a_{0}\right)^{2} \times\left(0.5 a_{0}\right)}{\left(0.5 a_{0}\right) \times\left(0.5 a_{0}\right)}=4{/tex}

questions.question:
Two solids dissociate as follows:
{tex}A(s) \rightleftharpoons B(g)+\mathrm{C}(g) ; K_{p_{1}}=x \ \mathrm{atm}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}D(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}(g)+E(g) ; K_{p_{2}}=y \ \mathrm{atm}^{2}{/tex}
The total pressure when both the solids dissociate simultaneously is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The equilibrium reaction for the dissociation of two solids is given as:
{tex}2(\sqrt{x+y}) \text { atm }{/tex}
{tex}\underset{Atequilibrium}\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;A(s)\rightleftharpoons\underset{P_1}{B(g)}+\underset{P_1+P_2}{C(g)}{/tex}
{tex} \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ K_{p_{1}}=x=p_{B} \cdot p_{C}=p_{1}\left(p_{1}+p_{2}\right) \\ Similarly, D(s) \rightleftharpoons C(g)+E(g){/tex} ...(i)
At equilibrium {tex}p_{1}+p_{2} \cdot p_{2}{/tex}
{tex}K_{p_{2}}=y= p_C \cdot p_E |=\left(p_{1}+p_{2}\right) p_{2}{/tex} ...(ii)
On adding Eq. (i) and (ii), we get.
KP1 + Kp2 = x + y = p1 (p1 + P2) + p2(p1 + p2)
= (p1 + p2)2
or {tex}\sqrt{x+y}=p_{1}+p_{2}{/tex} ...(iii)
Now, total pressure is given as
PT = PB + PC + PE
= P1 + (P1 + P2)+ P2
= 2(p1 + p2)
On substituting the value of p1 + p2 from Eq. (iii) to Eq. (iv), we get
{tex}p_{T}=2 \sqrt{x+y}{/tex}

questions.question:
Consider the reaction,
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g){/tex}
The equilibrium constant of the above reaction is Kp. If pure ammonia is left to dissociate, the partial pressure of ammonia at equilibrium is given by (Assume that {tex}p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}<<p_{\text {total }}{/tex} at equilibrium)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g){/tex}
At equilibrium: pNi = P, pHl = 3P, 2NH 3<g) Pnh 3 = 2-P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p(total) = {tex}p_{\mathrm{N}_{2}}+p_{\mathrm{H}_{2}}+p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}\simeq p_{\mathrm{N}_{2}}+p_{\mathrm{H}_{2}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} p(total) >> {tex}p_{NH_3}{/tex}]
= p + 3p = 4p
Now, {tex}K_{p}=\frac{p^{2} \mathrm{NH}_{3}}{p_{\mathrm{N}_{2}} \times p_{\mathrm{H}_{2}}^{3}}=\frac{p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{2}}{p \times(3 p)^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{2}}{27 \times p^{4}}=\frac{p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{2}}{27 \times\left(\frac{P}{4}\right)^{4}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} p = 4p]
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{2} \times 4^{4}}{3^{2} \times 3 \times P^{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{2}=\frac{3^{2} \times 3 \times P^{4} \times K_{p}}{4^{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}=\frac{3 \times 3^{1 / 2} \times P^{2} \times K_{p}^{1 / 2}}{4^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3^{3 / 2} K_{p}^{1 / 2} P^{2}}{16}{/tex}

questions.question: 5.1 g NH4SH is introduced in 3.0 L evacuated flask at 327°C. 30% of the solid NH4SH decomposed to NH3 and H2S as gases. The K of the reaction at 327° C is (R = 0.082 atm mol-1 K-1 , molar mass of S = 32g mol-1, molar mass of N = 14 g mol-1)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Molar mass of NH4SH = 18 + 33 = 51 g mol-1
Number of moles of NH4SH introduced in the vessel {tex}=\frac{\text { Weight }}{\text { Molar mass }}=\frac{5 \cdot 1}{51}=0.1 \mathrm{mol}{/tex}

{tex}K_{C}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{HS}(s)\right]}=\frac{0.01 \times 0.01}{1}=10^{-4}\left(\mathrm{mol} \mathrm{L}^{-1}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad K_{p}=K_{C}(R T)^{\Delta_{n_g}}{/tex}
[where, {tex}\Delta n_{g}=\Sigma n_{\text {product }}-\Sigma n_{\text {reactant }}{/tex}] = 2 - 0 = 2
{tex}\therefore \quad K_{p}=K_{C}(R T)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=10^{-4} \times[0.082 \times(273+327)]^{2} \mathrm{atm}^{2}{/tex}
= 0.242 atm2

questions.question:
The values of {tex} \frac{K_{p}}{K_{C}}{/tex} for the following reactions at 300 K are, respectively (At 300 K, RT= 24.62dm3 atm mol-1)
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+\mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}(g){/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g){/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g){/tex}

options.option: 1, 4.1 $\times$ 10^2 dm^-3 atm^-1 mol, 606 dm^6 atm^2 mol^-2
1, 24.62 dm^3 atm mol^-1, 606.0 dm^6 atm^2 mol^2

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
We know that, the relationship between K and K of a chemical equilibrium state (reaction) is
{tex}K_{p}=K_{C}(R T)^{\Delta n_{g}} \Rightarrow \frac{K_{p}}{K_{C}}=(R T)^{\Delta n_{g}}{/tex}
where, {tex}\Delta n_{g}=\Sigma n_{\text {products }}-\Sigma n_{\text {Reactants }}{/tex}

  1. {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+\mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}(g){/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (RT)2 - (1 + 1) = (RT)0 = 1
  2. {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g){/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (RT)2 - 1= RT = 24.62dm3 atm mol-1
  3. {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g){/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (RT)2 - (3 + 1) = (RT)-2
    {tex}=\frac{1}{\left(24.62 \mathrm{dm}^{3} \mathrm{atm} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
    = 1.65 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 dm-6 atm-2 mol2

questions.question:
Consider the following reversible chemical reactions,
{tex}A_{2}(g)+B_{2}(g) \stackrel{K_{1}}{\rightleftharpoons} 2 A B(g){/tex} ...(i)
{tex}6 A B(g) \stackrel{K_{2}}{\rightleftharpoons} 3 A_{2}(g)+3 B_{2}(g){/tex} ...(ii)
The relation between K1 and K2 is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
1. {tex}A_{2}(g)+B_{2}(g) \stackrel{K_{1}}{\rightleftharpoons} 2 A B(g){/tex} ; {tex}K_{1}=\frac{[A B]^{2}}{\left[A_{2}\right]\left[B_{2}\right]}{/tex}
  2. {tex}6 A B(g) \stackrel{K_{2}}{\rightleftharpoons} 3 A_{2}(g)+3 B_{2}(g){/tex} ; {tex}K_{2}=\frac{\left[A_{2}\right]^{3}\left[B_{2}\right]^{3}}{[A B]^6}=\frac{1}{\left(\frac{[A B]^{2}}{\left[A_{2}\right]\left[B_{2}\right]}\right)^{3}}=\frac{1}{K_{1}^{3}}{/tex},
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} K2 = K1-3

questions.question: An aqueous solution contains 0.10 M H2S and 0.20 M HCl. If the equilibrium constants for the formation of H S- from H2S is 1.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-7 and that of S2- from HS- ions is 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-13 then the concentration of S2- ions in aqueous solution is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Given [H2S] = 0.10 M
[HCI] = 0.20 M So, [H+] = 0.02 M
{tex}\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{HS^-}, K_{1}=1.0 \times 10^{-7}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{HS} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{S}^{2-}, K_{2}=1.2 \times 10^{-13}{/tex}
It means for,
{tex}\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{S}^{2-}{/tex}
{tex}K=K_{1} \times K_{2}=10 \times 10^{-7} \times 1.2 \times 10^{-13}{/tex}
{tex}=1.2 \times 10^{-20}{/tex}
Now, [S2-] = {tex}\frac{K \times\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^{2}}{/tex} [according to the final equation]
{tex}=\frac{1.2 \times 10^{-20} \times 0.1 \mathrm{M}}{(0.2 \mathrm{M})^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1.2 \times 10^{-20} \times 1 \times 10^{-1} \mathrm{M}}{4 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{M}}{/tex}
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-20M

questions.question: The equilibrium constant at 298 K for a reaction, {tex}A+B \rightleftharpoons C+D{/tex} is 100. If the initial concentrations of all the four species were 1 M each, then equilibrium concentration of D (in mol L-1) will be
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{\mathrm{eq}}=\frac{[C][D]}{[A][B]}=\frac{(1+x)(1+x)}{(1-x)(1-x)}=\frac{(1+x)^{2}}{(1-x)^{2}}{/tex}
or {tex}100=\left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right)^{2} \text { or } 10=\frac{1+x}{1-x}{/tex}
or 10 -10x = 1 + x
10 - 1 = x + 10x
9 = 11x
{tex}x=\frac{9}{11}=0.818{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} [D] = 1 + x = 1 + 0.818 = 1.818

questions.question: For the reaction, SO2(g) + {tex}\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons{/tex} SO3(g) if Kp = Kc(RT)x where, the symbols have usual meaning, then the value of x is (assuming ideality)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For the given reaction, {tex}\Delta{/tex}ng = nP - nR
where, nP = number of moles of products
nR = number of moles of reactants {tex}K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{\Delta n_{g}}{/tex}
{tex}\Delta n_{g}=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Consider the following equilibrium in a closed container {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g){/tex}.  At a fixed temperature, the volume of the reaction container is halved. For this change, which of the following statements hold true regarding the equilibrium constant (Kp) and degree of dissociation ({tex}\alpha{/tex})?

options.option: K_p changes but $\alpha$ does not change
K_p does not change but $\alpha$ changes

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
At constant temperature Kp or Kc remains constant. For the equilibria:
N2O4(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g)
{tex}\triangle n{/tex} = 1
Since, temperature is constant so Kc or Kp will remain constant. Further, since volume is halved, the pressure will be doubled so {tex}\alpha{/tex} will decrease so as to maintain the constancy of Kc or Kp.
From Le-Chatelier's principle reaction will move backwards.

questions.question: At constant temperature, the equilibrium constant (Kp) for the decomposition reaction, {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}{/tex}, is expressed by {tex}K_{p}=\frac{4 x^{2} p}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)}{/tex}, where, p = pressure, x = extent of decomposition. Which one of the following statement is true?

options.option: K_p increases with increase of p
K_p increases with decrease of x
K_p increases with increase of x
K_p remains constant with change in p and x

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}{/tex}, {tex}K_{p}=\frac{4 x^{2} p}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)}{/tex}, Kp is function of temperature only, does not change with either p or x

questions.question: For the reversible reaction, {tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g){/tex} at 500° C, the value of Kp is 1.44 {tex}\times{/tex}10-5 when partial pressure is measured in atmosphere. The corresponding value of Kc with concentration in mol/L is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g){/tex} {tex}\Delta n=-2{/tex}
{tex}K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{\Delta n}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{K_{p}}{(R T)^{\Delta n}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1.44 \times 10^{-5}}{(0.082 \times 773)^{-2}}{/tex}

questions.question: One mole of N2O4 (g) at 300 K is kept in a closed container under one atmosphere. It is heated to 600 K when 20% by mass of N2O4(g) decomposes to NO2(g). The resultant pressure is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: Total pressure = 2.40 atm

questions.question: An example of a reversible reaction is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
In all other reactions, at least one of the product is either insoluble precipitate or gas that drive the reaction continuously to right and do not allow equilibrium to be established. Following is the reversible reaction.
KNO3(aq ) + NaCl (aq ) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} KCl(aq )+ NaNO3(aq)

questions.question:
For the reaction, 1g mole of {tex}CaC{O_3}{/tex} is enclosed in 5L container
{tex}CaC{O_3}\left( s \right)\; \to CaO\left( s \right) + C{O_2}\left( g \right){/tex}
{tex}{K_p}{/tex} = 1.16 at 1073 K then per cent dissociation of {tex}CaC{O_3}{/tex} is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\underset{C(1-\alpha)}{\underset C{CaCO_3(s)}}\;\leftrightarrow\underset{C\alpha}{\underset0{\;CaO(s)\;}}+\underset{C\alpha}{\underset0{\;CO_2(g)}}{/tex}
{tex}K_p\;=\;{K_c(RT)}^{\bigtriangleup n}{/tex}
For the reaction, Δn = 1
So, Kp = Kc RT
{tex}or, K_c=\frac{K_p}{ RT}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1.16}{0.0821×1073}{/tex}
=0.0132
For the reaction, 
{tex}K_c = \frac{[CaO][CO_2]}{[CaCO_3]}{/tex}
Also, since CaCO3 and CaO are solids
So, [CaCO3] = [CaO] = 1
or, Kc= [CO2]
or, Cα=Kc
or, {tex}α=\frac{K_c}{C}{/tex}
Here, {tex}C=\frac{Mass}{Volume}=\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
So, {tex}α=\frac{0.0132×5}{1}=0.0658{/tex}
or, % α = 0.0658 ×100 = 6.58%

questions.question: The law of equilibrium was first given by
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
According to Guldberg and Waage, the rate at which substance reacts is directly proportional to its active mass (i.e. molar concentration).
{tex}aA\;+\;bB\Leftrightarrow\;xX\;+\;yY{/tex}
{tex}K_c=\frac{{\lbrack X\rbrack}^x{\lbrack Y\rbrack}^y}{{\lbrack A\rbrack}^a{\lbrack B\rbrack}^b}{/tex}
Kc = Equilibrium constant

questions.question: In which of the following reaction {tex}{K_p} < {K_c}{/tex}?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Kp​ is less than KC​ for {tex}2S{O_3} \to {{\text{O}}_2} + 2S{O_2}{/tex}
Δn= number of moles of gaseous products − number of moles of gaseous reactants
Δn=2−(2+1)
Δn=−1
Kp​=Kc​(RT)Δn
Kp​=Kc​(RT)−1
{tex}Kp​=\frac{K_c​​}{RT}{/tex}
{tex}RT > 1{/tex}
{tex}\dfrac {1 }{RT} <1{/tex}
{tex} \therefore K_p < K_c{/tex}

questions.question: The equilibrium constant of a reaction is 300. If the volume of a reaction flask is trippled, the equilibrium constant will be
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: As we know, equilibrium constant depends only upon temperature and here temperature is constant. So, the equilibrium constant will be the same and will not change with any change in the volume.

questions.question: If active mass of a 6% solution of a compound is 2, its molecular weight will be
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
6% of the solution contains 6g of the compound in 100ml of solution.
Mass of compound present in 1 liter of solution = 60g
Concentration = Moles/liter
= 60/Molar mass
Given active mass = 2
So, 60/Molar mass = 2
=> Molar mass = 30

questions.question: An equilibrium mixture of the reaction {tex}2{H_2}S\left( g \right) \rightleftharpoons 2{H_2}\left( g \right) + {S_2}\left( g \right),{/tex} had 0.5 mole{tex}{H_2}S{/tex}, 0.10 mole {tex}{H_2}{/tex} and 0.4 mole {tex}{S_2}{/tex} in one litre vessel. The value of equilibrium constant (K) in mole litre-1 is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K=\frac{\left[H_{2}\right]^{2}\left[S_{2}\right]}{\left[H_{2} S\right]^{2}}=\frac{[0.10]^{2}[0.4]}{[0.5]^{2}}=0.016{/tex}

questions.question: 2 moles of {tex}PC{l_5}{/tex} were introduced in a 2 litre flask and heated at 625 K to establish equilibrium, when 60% {tex}PC{l_5}{/tex} dissociated into {tex}PC{l_5}{/tex} and {tex}C{l_2}{/tex}. {tex}{K_c}{/tex}(equilibrium constant) will be
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The given reaction is:
{tex}\;\underset{At\;equilb.}{\underset{Initial}{}}\underset{C(1-\alpha)}{\underset C{PCl_5}}\Leftrightarrow\underset{C\alpha}{\underset0{PCl_{3\;}}}+\underset{C\alpha}{\underset0{Cl_2}}{/tex}
Initial 100 concentration
[∵ Initial concentration of {tex}PCl_5​=\frac{2mole​}{2L}=1M/L{/tex}]
Since 60% of PCl5​ is dissociated at equilibrium i.e. {tex}\therefore \alpha = \frac{60}{100} =0.60{/tex}
So, 40% of PCl5​ would be remaining at equilibrium
Now, {tex}K=\frac{[PCl_3​][Cl_2​]​}{[PCl_5​]}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{0.6×0.6}{0.4​}=0.09×10=0.9{/tex}

questions.question:
A vessel of one litre capacity containing 1 mole of {tex}S{O_3}{/tex} is heated till a state of equilibrium is attained.
{tex}2S{O_3}\left( g \right) \rightleftharpoons 2S{O_2}\left( g \right) + {{\text{O}}_2}\left( g \right).{/tex}
At equilibrium, 0.6 moles of {tex}S{O_2}{/tex} had formed. The value of equilibrium constant is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}​\underset{\;At\;Equilibrium}{\underset{Initial}{}}​\underset{0.4\;mole}{\underset{1\;mole}{2SO_{3(g)}}}\;\Leftrightarrow\underset{0.6mole}{\underset{0\;mole}{2SO_{2(g)}}}\;+\underset{0.3\;mole}{\;\underset{0\;mole}{O_{2(g)}}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow K_{equilb.} = \frac{[SO_2]^2[O_2]}{[SO_3]^2} = \frac{(0.6)^2(0.3)}{(0.4)^2} = 0.675{/tex}

questions.question: For reaction, {tex}2NOCl\left( g \right) = 2NO\left( g \right) + C{l_2}\left( g \right),{\text{ }}{K_c}{/tex} at {tex}427^\circ \,C{/tex} is {tex}3 \times {10^{ - 6}}L{\text{ }}mo{l^{ - 1}}.{/tex} The value of {tex}{K_p}{/tex} is nearly
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For the reaction given below, the value of Kp is
{tex}2 \mathrm{NOCl}_{(\mathrm{g})} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{(\mathrm{g})}+\mathrm{Cl}_{2(\mathrm{g})}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}}=\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{c}}(\mathrm{RT})^{\Delta \mathrm{n}}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{p}} = 3 \times 10^{-6}(0.0821 \times 700)=172.41 \times 10^{-6}=1.72 \times10^{-4}{/tex}

questions.question: We know that the relationship between Kc and Kp is Kp = Kc (RT){tex}\Delta{/tex}n. What would be the value of {tex}\Delta{/tex}n for the reaction NH4CIO) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} NH3(g) + HCl (g)?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
NH4Cl(s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} NH3(g) + HCl(g)
{tex}\Delta n(g){/tex} = 2 - 0 = 2

questions.question: PCl5, PCl3 and Cl2 are at equilibrium at 500 K in a closed container and their concentrations are 0.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1, 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1 and 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1 respectively. The value of Kc for the reaction PCl5(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl3(g) + Cl2(g) will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
At eq. {tex}\mathop {{\text{PC}}{{\text{l}}_5}(g)}\limits_{0.8 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {{\text{PC}}{{\text{l}}_3}}\limits_{1.2 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} + \mathop {{\text{C}}{{\text{l}}_2}}\limits_{1.2 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}} {/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{PCl}_{3}\right]\left[\mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{PCl}_{5}\right]}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1.2 \times 10^{-3} \times 1.2 \times 10^{-3}}{0.8 \times 10^{-3}}{/tex} = 1.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3

questions.question:
At 500 K, equilibrium constant, {tex}{{\text{K}}_{\text{c}}}{/tex}, for the following reaction is 5:
{tex}\frac{1}{2}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}\left( {\text{g}} \right) + \frac{1}{2}{\text{ }}{{\text{I}}_{\text{2}}}\left( {\text{g}} \right) \rightleftharpoons {/tex} HI (g)
What would be the equilibrium constant Kc for the reaction {tex}2HI(g) \rightleftharpoons {H_2}(g) + {I_2}(g){/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{I}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{HI}(g) ; K_{1}=5=\frac{[\mathrm{HI}]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}\left[\mathrm{I}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{H}_{2}(g)+\mathrm{I}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{HI}(g) ; K_{2}=\frac{[\mathrm{HI}]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{II}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{I}_{2}\right]}=K_{1}^{2}=25{/tex}
{tex}2 \mathrm{HI}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \mathrm{I}_{2}(g) ; K_{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{I}_{2}\right]}{[\mathrm{HI}]^{2}}=\frac{1}{25}{/tex} = 0.04

questions.question: Which of the following options will be correct for the stage of half completion of the reaction A {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} B?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
A {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} B
At half completion of reaction, [A] = [B]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Q =1
Now, {tex}\Delta G^{\ominus}{/tex} = -RT ln Q = -RT ln 1
= 0

questions.question: The reaction, A + 2B {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2C +D was studied using an initial concentration of B which was 1.5 times that of A. But the equilibrium concentration of A and C were found to be equal. Then the Kc for the equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop A\limits_a }\limits_{(a - x)} + \mathop {\mathop {2B}\limits_{\frac{3}{2}a} }\limits_{\left( {\frac{3}{2}a - 2x} \right)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2C}\limits_0 }\limits_{2x} + \mathop {\mathop D\limits_0 }\limits_x {/tex}
Given, a - x = 2x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x = \frac {a}{3}{/tex}
Now, {tex}K_{c}=\frac{[C]^{2}[D]}{[A][B]^{2}}=\frac{\left(\frac{2 a}{3}\right)^{2} \times \frac{a}{3}}{\left(a-\frac{a}{3}\right)\left(\frac{3 a}{2}-\frac{2 a}{3}\right)^{2}}{/tex} =  0.32

questions.question: One mole of nitrogen is mixed with 3 mole of hydrogen in a closed 3 litre vessel. 20% of nitrogen is converted into NH3. Then Kc for the {tex}\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{N}_{2}+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{H}_{2} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NH}_{3} {/tex} is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\mathop {\frac{1}{2}{{\text{N}}_2}}\limits_{\left( {1 - \frac{{20}}{{100}}} \right)} + \mathop {\frac{3}{2}{{\text{H}}_2}}\limits_{\left( {3 - \frac{{3 \times 20}}{{100}}} \right)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {{\text{N}}{{\text{H}}_3}}\limits_{\left( {\frac{{20 \times 2}}{{100}}} \right){\text{mole}}\;{\text{at}}\;{\text{eq}}{\text{.}}} }{/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{3 / 2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{[20 \times 2 /(100 \times 3)]}{\left[\frac{80}{100 \times 3}\right]^{1 / 2}\left[\frac{240}{100 \times 3}\right]^{3 / 2}}{/tex} = 0.36 L mol-1

questions.question: The correct relationship between Kc and Kp in gaseous equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=K_{c} \times(R T)^{\Delta n}{/tex}
where {tex}\Delta n{/tex} = mole of products - mole of reactants.

questions.question:
At constant temperature in one-litre vessel, when the reaction,
2SO3(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2SO2(g) + O2(g)
is at equilibrium, the SO2 concentration is 0.6 M, initial concentration of SO3 is 1 M. The equilibrium constant is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because \mathop {2S{O_3}}\limits_{(1 - 0.6)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {2S{O_2}}\limits_{0.6} + \mathop {{O_2}}\limits_{0.3} {/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_{2}\right]^{2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_{3}\right]^{2}}=\frac{0.6 \times 0.6 \times 0.3}{0.4 \times 0.4}{/tex} = 0.675

questions.question: One mole of ethyl alcohol was treated with one mole of acetic acid at 25°C. 2/3 of the acid changes into ester at equilibrium. The equilibrium constant for the reaction will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {C{H_2}COOH}\limits_1 }\limits_{(1 - 2/3)} + \mathop {\mathop {{C_2}{H_2}OH}\limits_1 }\limits_{(1 - 2/3)} \to {/tex}{tex}C{H_2}\mathop {\mathop {COO{C_2}}\limits_0 }\limits_{2/3} {H_5} + \mathop {\mathop {{H_2}O}\limits_0 }\limits_{2/3} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{c}=\frac{\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{2}{3}}{\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3}}=4{/tex}

questions.question: For N2 + 3H2 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NH3; {tex}\Delta{/tex}H = -ve then:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=K_{c}[R T]^{\Delta n}{/tex}
{tex}\Delta n{/tex} = Mole of products - mole of reactants = 2 - 4 = -2

questions.question: The correct relation between Kp and Kc for the reaction aX + bY {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} bZ + aW is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}\Delta{/tex}n = (a + b) - (a + b) = 0

questions.question:
The equilibrium constant Kp for the given reaction is 3.5 atm-1 at 1000K.
{tex}\mathop {2S{O_2}}\limits_{(g)} + \mathop {{O_2}}\limits_{(g)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {2S{O_3}}\limits_{(g)} {/tex}
The partial pressure of oxygen gas which results in equal mole of SO2 and SO3 at equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{\left(P_{\mathrm{SO}_{3}}\right)^{2}}{\left(P_{\mathrm{SO}_{2}}\right)^{2} \times P_{\mathrm{O}_{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\because \ P_{\mathrm{SO}_{3}}=P_{\mathrm{SO}_{2}}{/tex} at equilibrium
{tex}3.5=\frac{1}{P_{\mathrm{O}_{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ P_{\mathrm{O}_{2}}=\frac{1}{3.5}{/tex} = 0.29 atm

questions.question: For the reaction, {tex}\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(s){/tex} + 2H2O(v). Which one is correct representation?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{p}=\left(p_{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}}\right)^{2}{/tex} and Kc = [H2O]2; the solid species are not used in writing Kc or Kp.

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants for the reactions are:
H3PO4 {tex}\overset{K_1}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + H2P{tex}\text O_4^{-}{/tex}; K1
H2PO4  {tex}\overset{K_2}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + HP{tex}\text O_4^{2-}{/tex}; K2
HPO4  {tex}\overset{K_3}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + P{tex}\text O_4^{3-}{/tex}; K3
The equilibrium constant for
{tex}\mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4} \rightleftharpoons 3 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-}{/tex} will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{1}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{PO}_{4}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{2}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{HPO}_{4}^{2-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{PO}_{4}^{-}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HPO}_{4}^{2-}\right]}{/tex}
Multiplying these, three, K {tex}=K_{1} \times K_{2} \times K_{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^{3}\left[\mathrm{PO}_{4}^{3-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{PO}_{4}\right]}{/tex}.

questions.question: In {tex}K_{p}=K_{c}[R T]^{\Delta n}, \Delta n{/tex} may have:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}\Delta n{/tex} may be zero, positive or negative integers or fractional depending upon nature of reaction. {tex}\Delta n{/tex} = No. of mole of products - No. of mole of reactants (count only gaseous phase).

questions.question: Active mass is given as:

options.option: amount of a substance per unit volume
number of g mol per 100 litre

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Active mass = activity coefficient (f) {tex}\times{/tex} molarity (CM)
or Active mass = activity coefficient (r) {tex}\times{/tex} molality (CM)

questions.question: For a reversible reaction, the rate constant for the forward reaction is 2.38 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 and for the backward reaction is 8.15 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5. The Kc for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{c}=\frac{k_{f}}{k_{b}}=\frac{2.38 \times 10^{-4}}{8.15 \times 10^{-5}}{/tex} = 2.92

questions.question: At temperature, T, a compound AB2(g) dissociates according to the reaction 2AB2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2AB(g) + B2 (g) with a degree of dissociation x which is small compared with unity. The expression for Kp in terms of x and the total pressure, P is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {2A{B_2}}\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - x} (g) \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2AB(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_x + \mathop {\mathop {{B_2}(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_{\frac{x}{2}} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad K_{p}=\frac{x^{2} \cdot x}{2(1-x)^{2}}\left[\frac{P}{1+\frac{x}{2}}\right]^{1}{/tex}
x being small
{tex}\therefore \quad 1-x=1{/tex} and {tex}1+\frac{x}{2}=1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad K_{p}=\frac{x^{3} \cdot P}{2}{/tex}

questions.question:
One mole of SO3 in one litre vessel attains equilibrium at T K:
2SO3(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2SO2(g) + O2(g)
The equilibrium mixture required 0.2 mole KMnO4 in acid medium. What is Kc for:
{tex}\mathrm{SO}_{3} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}{/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Eq. of SO2 formed = eq. of KMnO4 used
{tex}=0.2 \times 5=1.0^{-}{/tex} (Mn7+ + 5e + Mn2+)
mole of SO2 formed {tex}=\frac{1.0}{2}=0.5{/tex} {tex}\left(\mathrm{S}^{4+} \rightarrow \mathrm{S}^{6+}+2 e\right){/tex}
{tex}\begin{array}{ccc} 2 \mathrm{SO}_{3}(g) & \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{SO}_{2}(\mathrm{~g})+ \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \\ 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0.5 & 0.5 & 0.25 \end{array}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c_{1}}=\frac{(0.5)^{2} \times 0.25}{(0.5)^{2}}=0.25{/tex}
{tex}\therefore K_{c_{2}} \text { for } \mathrm{SO}_{3} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c_{2}}=\sqrt{K_{c_{1}}}=\sqrt{0.25}{/tex} = 0.5

questions.question: A mixture of nitrogen and hydrogen in the ratio of 1 : 3 reach equilibrium with ammonia when 50% of the mixture has reacted. If the total pressure is P, the partial pressure of ammonia in the equilibrium mixture was:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {{N_2}}\limits_1 }\limits_{(1 - x)} + \mathop {\mathop {3{H_2}}\limits_3 }\limits_{(3 - 3x)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2N{H_3}}\limits_0 }\limits_{2x} {/tex} x = 0.5
Total mole at eq. = 0.5 + 1.5 + 1.0 = 3
Thus {tex}P_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}=P_{T} \times{/tex} mole fraction of NH3 {tex}=P \times \frac{1}{3}=\frac P 3{/tex}

questions.question: For NH4HS(s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} NH3(g) + H2S(g), the observed pressure for reaction mixture in equilibrium is 1.12 atm at 106oC. The value of Kp for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {}\limits_{\Pr essure\;at\;equilibrium} N{H_4}HS(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathop {N{H_3}(g)}\limits_P + \mathop {{H_2}S(g)}\limits_P {/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total pressure at equilibrium = 2P = 1.12 atm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = {tex}\frac {1.12}2{/tex} atm
{tex}\because \ K_{p}=p_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{\prime} \times p_{\mathrm{H}_{2}}^{\prime}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{p}=\frac{1.12}{2} \times \frac{1.12}{2}=0.3136 \mathrm{~atm}^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: For reaction: PCl3(g) + Cl2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl5 (g), the value of Kc at 250°C is 26 mol litre-1. The value of Kp at this temperature will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{\Delta n}{/tex},
{tex}\because \Delta n=-1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore K_{p}=26 \times(0.0821 \times 523)^{-1}{/tex} = 0.61 atm-1

questions.question: At a given temperature the Kc for the reaction, PCl5(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl3 (g) + Cl2 (g) is 2.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3. At the same temperature, the Kc for the reaction PCl3(g) + Cl2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl5(g) is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{c_{1}}=\frac{1}{K_{c_{2}}}=\frac{1}{2.4 \times 10^{-3}}=4.2 \times 10^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: For the reaction, C(s) + CO2 (g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2CO(g), the partial pressures of CO2 and CO are 2.0 and 4.0 atm respectively at equilibrium. The Kp for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{p}=\frac{\left(p_{\mathrm{CO}}\right)^{2}}{p_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}}=\frac{4 \times 4}{2}=8{/tex}

questions.question:
For the reaction in equilibrium,
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g){/tex}
the concentrations, of N2O4 and NO2 at equilibrium are {tex}4.8 \times 10^{-2}{/tex} and {tex}1.2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1}{/tex} respectively. The value of Kc for this reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{NO}_{2}\right]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}\right]}=\frac{\left[1.2 \times 10^{-2}\right]^{2}}{4.8 \times 10^{-2}}{/tex}
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1

questions.question:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction:
P4(s) + 5O2 (g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} P4O10 (s) is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{c}=\frac{1}{\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{5}}{/tex}; note that the expression does not involve the concentration terms of solid species.

questions.question: In aqueous solution the ionization constants for carbonic acid are K1 = 4.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-7 and K2 = 4.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-11. Select the correct statement for a saturated 0.034 M solution of the carbonic acid.

options.option: The concentration of H^+ is double that of $\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}$
The concentrations of H^+ and $\mathrm{HCO}_{3}^{-}$ are approximately equal
The concentration of $\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}$ is greater than that of $\mathrm{HCO}_{3}^{-}$

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
H2CO3 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + {tex}\mathrm{HCO}_{3}^{-}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{HCO}_{3}^{-}{/tex} {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + {tex}\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}{/tex} (weak dissociation)

questions.question: For the reaction, CO(g) + Cl2 (g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} COCl2 (g) the value of Kp/Kc is equal to:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta{/tex} = 1 - 2 = -1
{tex}\therefore \ K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{\Delta n}{/tex}
Kp = Kc(RT)-1

questions.question:
The equilibrium constant (Kc) for the reaction,
N2(g) + O2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO(g)
at room temperature T is 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4. The value of Kc for {tex}\mathrm{NO}(g) \rightleftharpoons \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g){/tex} at the same T is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c_{1}}=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]}=4 \times 10^{-4}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c_{2}}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}}{[\mathrm{NO}]}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{c_{2}}=\sqrt{\frac{1}{K_{c_{1}}}}=\sqrt{\frac{1}{4 \times 10^{-4}}}{/tex} = 50

questions.question: For the reaction: 2NO2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO (g) + O2(g); Kc = 1.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6 at 184oC and R = 0.083 litre atm K-1 mol-1. When Kp and Kc are compared at 184°C, it is found that:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta n{/tex} = 2 + 1 - 2 = +1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Kp = Kc(RT)1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Kp > Kc

questions.question: A definite amount of solid NH4HS is placed in a flask already containing NH3 gas at certain temperature and 0.50 atm pressure. NH4HS decomposes to give NH3 and H2S and total equilibrium pressure in flask is 0.84 atm. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\begin{array}{cccc} & \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{HS}(s) & \rightleftharpoons & \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g)+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(g) \\ \mathrm{at} & t=0 & & 0.5 & 0 \\ \mathrm{at} & t=\mathrm{eq} . & & 0.5+P & P \end{array}{/tex}
PT = 0.5 + P + P = 0.84
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = 0.17 atm
Kp = {tex}P_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}} \times P_{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}}{/tex} = (0.5 + 0.17) {tex}\times{/tex} 0.17
= 0.11 atm

questions.question:
Phosphorus pentachloride dissociates as follows, in a closed reaction vessel,
PCl5 (g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl3 (g) + Cl2 (g)
If total pressure at equilibrium of the reaction mixture is P and degree of dissociation of PCl5 is x, the partial pressure of PCl3 will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {PC{l_5}}\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - x} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {PC{l_3}}\limits_0 }\limits_x + \mathop {\mathop {C{l_2}}\limits_0 }\limits_x {/tex}
{tex}\therefore P_{\mathrm{PCl}_{5}}^{\prime}=P_{T} \times \mathrm{~m} . \mathrm{f}{/tex} of {tex}\mathrm{PCl}_{3}=P .\left[\frac{x}{1+x}\right]{/tex}

questions.question:
The equilibrium constant for the reaction,
{tex}\mathrm{SO}_{3}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) K_{c}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}{/tex}
The Kc for the reaction: 2SO2(g) + O2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2SO3(g) will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c_{1}}=4.9 \times 10^{-2}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_{3}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c_{2}}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{SO}_{3}\right]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{SO}_{2}\right]^{2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore K_{c_{2}}=\left[\frac{1}{K_{c_{1}}}\right]^{2}=\left[\frac{1}{4.9 \times 10^{-2}}\right]^{2}{/tex} = 416.5

questions.question:
For the following three reactions a, b and c equilibrium constants are given:

  1. CO(g) + H2O(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} CO2 (g) + H2(g); K1
  2. CH4 (g) + H2O(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} CO(g) + 3H2 (g); K2
  3. CH4(g) + 2H2O(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} CO2 (g) + 4H2 (g); K3

Which of the following relations is correct?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{1}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{CO}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]}{[\mathrm{CO}]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]}{/tex} ...(1)
{tex}K_{2}=\frac{[\mathrm{CO}]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{CH}_{4}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]}{/tex} ...(2)
{tex}K_{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{CO}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{4}}{\left[\mathrm{CH}_{4}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]}{/tex} ...(3)
By eqs. (1), (2) and (3)
{tex}K_{3}=K_{1} \times K_{2}{/tex}

questions.question: A vessel at 1000 K contains CO2 with a pressure of 0.5 atm. Some of the CO2 is converted into CO on the addition of graphite. If the total pressure at equilibrium is 0.8 atm, the value of K is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {C{O_2}}\limits_{0.5\;atm} }\limits_{0.5 - p} + C(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2CO(g)}\limits_{} }\limits_{2p} {/tex}
Total pressure = 0.5 - P + 2P = 0.8
P = 0.3
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{P_{\mathrm{CO}}^{2}}{P_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}}=\frac{(2 P)^{2}}{(0.5-P)}=\frac{(0.6)^{2}}{(0.5-0.3)}{/tex}
Kp = 1.8

questions.question: The equilibrium constant at 298 K for a reaction A + B {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} C + D is 100. If the initial concentration of all the four species were 1 M each, then equilibrium concentration of D (in mol L-1) will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{Concentration\;at\;t = 0} }\limits_{Concentration\,\;at\;eq.} \mathop {\mathop A\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - x} + \mathop {\mathop B\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - x} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop C\limits_1 }\limits_{1 + x} + \mathop {\mathop D\limits_1 }\limits_{1 + x} {/tex}
{tex}\because \quad K_{c}=\frac{[C][D]}{[A][B]}=\frac{(1+x)^{2}}{(1-x)^{2}}=100{/tex}
or {tex}\frac{1+x}{1-x}=10{/tex}
or 1 + x = 10 - 10x
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{9}{11}{/tex}
Now [D] = 1 + x {tex}=1+\frac{9}{11}{/tex} = 1.818

questions.question: For the reaction {tex}\mathrm{SO}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{3}(g){/tex} if Kp = KC(RT)x where the symbols have usual meaning then the value of x is: (assuming ideality)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}\Delta n_{(g)}{/tex} = (Number of product molecules - Number of reactant molecules) in gaseous state {tex}=1-\left(1+\frac{1}{2}\right)=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}.

questions.question: An aqueous solution contains 0.10 M H2S and 0.20 M HCl. If the equilibrium constant for the formation of HS- from H2S is 1.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-7 and that of S2- from HS- ions is 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-13 then the concentration of S2- ions in aqueous solution is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
H2S {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + HS-; {tex}\mathrm{Ka}_{1}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{II}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{HS}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right]}{/tex}
HS- {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + S2-; {tex}K a_{2}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{S}^{2-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HS}^{-}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} K1 {tex}\times{/tex} K2 = {tex}\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^{2}\left[\mathrm{S}^{2-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right]}{/tex}
or [S2-] = {tex}\frac{K_{1} \times K_{2} \times\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1.0 \times 10^{-7} \times 1.2 \times 10^{-13} \times 0.1}{(0.2)^{2}}{/tex} =  3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-20
[H+] are provided by strong acid as dissociation of weak acid H2S is suppressed in presence of strong acid HCl.

questions.question:
K1 and K2 are equilibrium constant for reactions (i) and (ii)
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2(g)}+\mathrm{O}_{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{(g)}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\mathrm{NO}_{(g)} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{N}_{2(g)}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2(g)}{/tex} ...(ii)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
N2(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g) K1
Reverse the reaction
2NO(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2(g) + O2(g), {tex}\frac {1}{K_1}{/tex}
Divide the above equation by 2
NO(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} N2(g) + O2(g) {tex}\left(\frac {1 }{ K_1}\right)^\frac {1 }{ 2}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\frac{1}{ K _1}\right)^\frac {1}{2}= K _2\left(\frac{1}{ K _1}\right)^\frac {1}{ 2}= K _2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow K _1=\left[\frac{1}{ K _2}\right]^2{/tex}

questions.question: 1.1 mole of A are mixed with 2.2 mole of B and the mixture is then kept in one litre flask till the equilibrium is attained A + 2B {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2C + D. At the equilibrium 0.2 mole of C are formed. The equilibrium constant of the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop A\limits_a }\limits_{(a - x)} + \mathop {\mathop {2B}\limits_b }\limits_{(b - 2x)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2C}\limits_0 }\limits_{2x} + \mathop {\mathop D\limits_0 }\limits_x {/tex}
Given, 2x = 0.2, Also, a = 1.1
x = 0.1, b = 2.2
{tex}\therefore K_{c}=\frac{(2 x)^{2} \cdot x}{(a-x)(b-2 x)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(2 \times 0.1)^{2} \times 0.1}{(1.1-0.1)(2.2-0.2)^{2}}{/tex} = 0.001

questions.question:
For the equilibrium,
MgCO3(s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} MgO(s) + CO2(g),
Which of the following expressions is correct?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: Among the given species involved in the reaction only gaseous species is CO2 thus, {tex}K_{p}=p_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}{/tex} [p = partial pressure]

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants for following reactions are given
N2 + 3H2 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NH3, K1
N2 + O2 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO, K2
H2 + {tex}\frac 12{/tex}O2 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} H2O, K3
The equilibrium constant (K) of the reaction, 2NH3 + {tex}\frac{5}{2} O _2 \rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO + 3H2O will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}{/tex}
{tex}K=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]^{2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^\frac {3 }{ 2}}=\frac{K_{2} \times K_{3}^{3}}{K_{1}}{/tex}

questions.question:
In the two gaseous reactions (i) and (ii) at 250oC

  1. {tex}\mathrm{NO}_{(g)}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NO}_{2(g)} , K_{1}{/tex}
  2. 2NO2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO(g) + O2(g), K2
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}NO _{(g)}+\frac{1}{2} O _{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons NO _{2(g)} K _1{/tex}
Reverse the above equation
{tex}NO _{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons NO _{(g)}+\frac{1}{2} O _{2(g)} \frac {1 }{ K _1}{/tex}
Multiply the above equation by 2
2NO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g) + O2(g) {tex}\left(\frac{1}{ K _1}\right)^2= K _2{/tex}

questions.question:
The reaction,
2A(g) + B(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 3C (g) + D(g)
is begun with the concentration of A and B both at an initial value of 1.00M. When equilibrium is reached, the concentration of D is measured and found to be 0.25. The value for the equilibrium constant for this reaction is given by the expression:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{Initial} }\limits_{At\;Eq.} \mathop {\mathop {2A(g)}\limits_{1.0} }\limits_{0.5} + \mathop {\mathop {2B(g)}\limits_{1.0} }\limits_{0.75} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {3C(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_{0.75} + \mathop {\mathop {D(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_{0.25} {/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{(0.25) \times(0.75)^{3}}{(0.5)^{2} \times(0.75)}{/tex}

questions.question: For which reaction {tex}K_{p} \neq K_{c}{/tex}?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta{/tex}n = 0 then, Kp = Kc
for (d) {tex}\Delta{/tex}n = 1

questions.question:
Given that the equilibrium constant for the reaction
2SO2(g) + O2( g ) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2SO3(g)
has a value of 278 at a particular temperature. What is the value of the equilibrium constant for the following reaction at the same temperature?
{tex}\mathrm{SO}_{3}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g){/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
2SO2 + O2 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2SO3; K = 278
{tex}\mathrm{SO}_{3} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{SO}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} ; \quad K^{\prime}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{K}}{/tex}
{tex}K^{\prime}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{K}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{278}}=6 \times 10^{-2}{/tex}

questions.question: The dissociation constant for acetic acid and HCN at 25oC are 1.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 and 4.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-10 respectively. The equilibrium constant for the equilibrium, CN- + CH3COOH {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} HCN + CH3COO- would be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
CH3COOH {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} CH3COO- + H+
Ka1 = 1.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 = {tex}\frac{\left[\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COO}^{-}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}\right]}{/tex} ...(i)
HCN {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H+ + CN-
Ka2 = 4.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-10 = {tex}\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{CN}^{-}\right]}{[\mathrm{HCN}]}{/tex} ...(ii)
By (i)/(ii)
K = {tex}\frac{K_{a_{1}}}{K_{a_{2}}}=\frac{[\mathrm{HCN}]\left[\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COO}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{CN}^{-}\right]\left[\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}\right]}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1.5 \times 10^{-5}}{4.5 \times 10^{-10}}{/tex}
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 104

questions.question:
Given the reaction between 2 gases represented by A2 and B2 to give the compound AB (g),
A2(g) + B2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2AB(g)
At equilibrium, the concentration
of {tex}A_{2}=3.0 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}{/tex}
of {tex}B_{2}=4.2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}{/tex}
of AB {tex}=2.8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}{/tex}
If the reaction takes place in a sealed vessel at 527°C, then the value of Kc will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{[A B]^{2}}{\left[A_{2}\right]\left[B_{2}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left(2.8 \times 10^{-3}\right)^{2}}{3 \times 10^{-3} \times 42 \times 10^{-3}}{/tex} = 0.62

questions.question: For a given exothermic reaction, Kp and K'p are the equilibrium constants at temperatures T1 and T2 respectively. Assuming that heat of reaction is constant in temperatures range between T1 and T2, it is readily observation that:(T2 > T1)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
2.303 log {tex}\frac{K_{p}^{\prime}}{K_{p}}=\frac{\Delta H}{R} \frac{\left[T_{2}-T_{1}\right]}{T_{1} T_{2}}{/tex}
Option {tex}K_{p}>K_{p}^{\prime}{/tex} is correct only if T2 > T1.

questions.question: For the reversible system: X(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} Y(g) + Z(g) a quantity of X was heated at constant pressure P at a certain temperature. The equilibrium partial pressure of X was found to be {tex}\frac P7{/tex}. What is the value of Kp at given temperature?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop X\limits_a }\limits_{a - x} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop Y\limits_0 }\limits_x + \mathop {\mathop Z\limits_0 }\limits_x {/tex}
Total mole at equilibrium = a - x + x + x = a + x
Thus {tex}P_{X}^{\prime}=P \frac{(a-x)}{(a+x)}=\frac{P}{7}{/tex} {tex}\therefore 7 a-7 x=a+x, \therefore x=\frac{6 a}{8}=\frac{3 a}{4}{/tex}
Also {tex}K_{p}=\frac{x^{2}}{(a-x)} \cdot\left[\frac{P}{a+x}\right]=\frac{9 a^{2} \times 4}{16 \times a} \times \frac{P \times 4}{7 a}=\frac{9 P}{7}{/tex}

questions.question: In a reaction at equilibrium 'x' moles of the reactant A decompose to give 1 mole each of C and D. If the fraction of A decomposed at equilibrium is independent of initial concentration of A, then the value of 'x' is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {xA}\limits_a }\limits_{a(1 - \alpha )} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop C\limits_0 }\limits_{\frac{{a\alpha }}{x}} + \mathop {\mathop X\limits_0 }\limits_{\frac{{a\alpha }}{x}} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad K=\frac{[C][D]}{[A]^{x}}=\frac{\frac{a^{2} \alpha^{2}}{x^{2} V^{2}}}{\frac{[a(1-\alpha)]^{x}}{V^{x}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\alpha^{2} a^{2-x}}{x^{2}(1-\alpha)^{x} \cdot V^{2-x}}{/tex}
If {tex}\alpha{/tex} is independent of a, then 2 - x = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2

questions.question:
Amount of PCl5 (in moles) need to be added to one litre vessel at 250°C in order to obtain a concentration of 0.1 mole of Cl2 for the given change is:
PCl5 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl3 + Cl2; KC = 0.0414
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{Initial\;moles} }\limits_{Moles\;at\;equilibrium} \mathop {\mathop {PC{l_5}}\limits_a }\limits_{(a - 0.1)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {PC{l_3}}\limits_0 }\limits_{0.1} + \mathop {\mathop {C{l_2}}\limits_0 }\limits_{0.1} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{c}=\frac{\frac{0.1}{1} \times \frac{0.1}{1}}{\frac{a-0.1}{1}}{/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} volume is 1 litre)
or {tex}0.0414=\frac{0.01}{(a-0.1)}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a = 0.3415 moles

questions.question: For the reaction, 2NO2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO(g) + O2(g), {tex}K_{c}=1.8 \times 10^{-6}{/tex} at 185oC. At 185oC, the Kc for {tex}\mathrm{NO}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g){/tex} is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c_{1}}=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{NO}_{2}\right]^{2}}=1.8 \times 10^{-6}{/tex}
Also, {tex}K_{c_{2}}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{NO}_{2}\right]}{[\mathrm{NO}]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c_{1}}=\frac{1}{K_{c_{2}}^{2}}{/tex}
or {tex}1.8 \times 10^{-6}=\frac{1}{K_{c_{2}}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore K_{c_{2}}=7.5 \times 10^{2}{/tex}

questions.question: A quantity of PCl5 was heated in a 10 litre vessel at 250°C to show PCl5(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl3(g) + Cl2(g). At equilibrium the vessel contains 0.1 mole of PCl5, 0.20 mole of PCl3 and 0.20 mole of Cl2. The equilibrium constant of the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{PCl}_{3}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{PCl}_{5}\right]}=\frac{\frac{0.2}{10} \times \frac{0.2}{10}}{\frac{0.1}{10}}{/tex} = 0.04

questions.question: At a certain temperature, 2HI {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} H2 + I2 only 50% HI is dissociated at equilibrium. The equilibrium constant is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{x^{2}}{4(1-x)^{2}}{/tex}
If, x = 0.5;
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{0.5 \times 0.5}{4 \times(0.5)^{2}}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} = 0.25

questions.question: Eight mole of a gas AB3 attain equilibrium in a closed container of volume 1dm3 as, 2 AB3 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} A2 (g) + 3B2(g). If at equilibrium 2 mole of A2 are present then, equilibrium constant is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{t = 0} }\limits_{At.\;equilibrium} \mathop {\mathop {2A{B_3}(g)}\limits_8 }\limits_{(8 - a)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {{A_2}(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_{at\;2} + \mathop {\mathop {3{B_2}(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_{\frac{{3a}}{2}} {/tex}
Thus, {tex}K_{c}=\frac{\left[A_{2}\right]\left[B_{2}\right]^{3}}{\left[A B_{3}\right]^{2}}{/tex}; Also, {tex}\frac{a}{2}=2{/tex}
{tex}\therefore a=4{/tex}
{tex}\therefore\left[A B_{3}\right]=\frac{4}{1} ;\left[A_{2}\right]=\frac{2}{1} ;\left[B_{2}\right]=\frac{6}{1}{/tex}
Thus, {tex}K_{c}=\frac{2 \times 6^{3}}{4^{2}}{/tex} = 27 mol2L-2

questions.question:
For the reversible reaction,
N2(g) + 3H2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NH3(g) at 500°C, the value of Kp is 1.44 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 when partial pressure is measured in the atmosphere. The corresponding value of Kc, with concentration in mol litre-1, is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{\Delta n};{/tex} {tex}\Delta n{/tex} for the given reaction is 2 - 4 = -2
R = 0.082 L atm K-1 mol-1
Thus, {tex}K_{c}=\frac{K_{p}}{(R T)^{\Delta n}}=\frac{1.44 \times 10^{-5}}{(0.082 \times 773)^{-2}}{/tex}

questions.question:
For the reaction:
{tex}\mathrm{CO}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CO}_{2}(g){/tex}, Kp/Kc is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta n=1-1-\frac{1}{2}=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{-1 / 2}{/tex}

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants of the following are:
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}{/tex}; K1
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}{/tex}; K2
{tex}\mathrm{H}_{2}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}{/tex}; K3
The equilibrium constant (K) of the reaction:
{tex}2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}+\frac{5}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \stackrel{K}{\rightleftharpoons} 2 \mathrm{NO}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O};{/tex} will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}2 \mathrm{NH}_{3} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{N}_{2}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}{/tex}; {tex}\frac{1}{K_{1}}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]^{2}}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}{/tex}; {tex}K_{2}=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]}{/tex} ...(ii)
{tex}\frac{3 \mathrm{H}_{2}+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} ; \quad K_{3}^{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{3}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{3 / 2}}}{2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}+\frac{5}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \stackrel{K}{\rightleftharpoons} 2 \mathrm{NO}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}}{/tex} ...(iii)
{tex}K=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]^{2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{5 / 2}}{/tex}
By multiplying (i), (ii), and (iii)
{tex}\frac{1}{K_{1}} \times K_{2} \times K_{3}^{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]^{2}} \times \frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]} \times \frac{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\right]^{3}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{3 / 2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2} \times\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]^{3}}{\left[\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right]^{2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{5 / 2}}=K{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K=\frac{K_{2} \times K_{3}^{3}}{K_{1}}{/tex}

questions.question: For the reaction: n1A(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} n2B(g), heat of reaction at constant pressure at 27°C is 2 kcal less than heat of reaction at constant volume, then:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta H=\Delta U+\Delta n R T{/tex}
{tex}\Delta H<\Delta U{/tex}
Thus, {tex}\Delta n=-\mathrm{ve}{/tex}
Now, {tex}K_{p}=K_{c}(R T)^{\Delta n}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{p}<K_{c}{/tex}

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants of given reactions are:
{tex}\mathrm{HgCl}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HgCl}_{2}{/tex}; {tex}K_{1}=3 \times 10^{6}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}{/tex}; K2 = 9.0
The equilibrium constant for disproportionation of HgCl2,
{tex}2 \mathrm{HgCl}_{2} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{HgCl}^{+}+\mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}{/tex} is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{1}=3 \times 10^{-5}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{2}=9.0=\frac{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{3}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}\right]\left[\mathrm{HgCl}^{+}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}\right]^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{3}=\frac{K_{2}}{K_{1}}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]} \times \frac{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}\right]^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}K_{3}=\frac{9}{3 \times 10^{6}}=3 \times 10^{-6}{/tex}

questions.question: 2 mole of A2 in one litre vessel are connected through a tube having a valve of negligible volume with 4 mole of B2 in 3 litre vessel at 27°C. The valve is opened to attain the equilibrium A2 + B2 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2AB. If Kc = 4, what is the concentration in mole litre-1 of AB?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {{A_2}}\limits_2 }\limits_{2 - x} + \mathop {\mathop {{B_2}}\limits_4 }\limits_{4 - x} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2AB}\limits_0 }\limits_{2x} {/tex}
Total volume = 4 litre
{tex}K_{c}=4=\frac{[A B]^{2}}{\left[A_{2}\right]\left[B_{2}\right]}=\frac{\left(\frac{2 x}{4}\right)^{2}}{\left(\frac{2-x}{4}\right) \times\left(\frac{4-x}{4}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}4=\frac{4 x^{2}}{x^{2}-6 x+8}{/tex}
or 24x = 32
{tex}\therefore \ x=\frac{32}{24}=\frac{4}{3}{/tex} = 0.33
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2x = 0.66
Thus, {tex}[A B]=\frac{0.66}{4}{/tex} = 0.165 mole litre-1

questions.question:
For the equilibrium:
NH2COONH4 (s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NH3 (g) + CO2 (g)
NH3 (g) is added in equilibrium mixture such that partial pressure of NH3 becomes equal to the original total pressure. The ratio of total pressure at second equilibrium to the original equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}N{H_2}COON{H_4} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {2N{H_3}}\limits_{2p} + \mathop {C{O_2}}\limits_p {/tex} ...(I)
At eq.
{tex}\therefore \quad K_{p}=(2 P)^{2} \times P=4 P^{3}{/tex}
{tex}N{H_2}COON{H_4} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {2N{H_3}}\limits_{3p} + \mathop {C{O_2}}\limits_a {/tex} ...(II)
{tex}K_{p}=(3 P)^{2} \times a{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad a=\frac{4 P^{3}}{9 P^{2}}=\frac{4 P}{9}{/tex}
Thus total pressure II eq. {tex}=3 P+\frac{4 P}{9}=\frac{31 P}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{P_{\mathrm{II}}}{P_{\mathrm{I}}}=\frac{31 P}{9 \times 3 P}=\frac{31}{27}{/tex}

questions.question:
Acetone undergoes dissociation as:
CH3COCH3(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} C2H6(g) + CO(g)
If the initial pressure of acetone is 150 mm at TK and at equilibrium at 27 K. the mole fraction of CO is 1/3, the value of Kp for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{Initial\;mole} }\limits_{Final\;mole} \mathop {\mathop {C{H_2}COC{H_3}(g)}\limits_a }\limits_{a - b} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {{C_2}{H_6}}\limits_0 }\limits_b + \mathop {\mathop {CO(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_b {/tex}
Given {tex}\frac{b}{a+b}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
a = 2ab
PV = nRT
{tex}\therefore P \propto T{/tex}, Thus at 2T initial pressure becomes 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 150 = 300 mm before dissociation.
For acetone before dissociation {tex}300 \times V=a \times R \times 2 T{/tex}
After dissociation: {tex}P \times V=(a+b) \times R \times 2 T{/tex}
{tex}\frac{P}{300}=\frac{(a+b)}{a}=\frac{\left(\frac{3 a}{2}\right)}{a}{/tex}
P = 450 mm
{tex}\therefore \ P_{\mathrm{CO}}^{\prime}=450 \times \frac{b}{a+b}{/tex} = 150 mm
{tex}P_{\mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{H}_{6}}^{\prime}=450 \times \frac{b}{a+b}{/tex} = 150 mm
{tex}P_{\mathrm{CH}, \mathrm{COCH}_{3}}^{\prime}=\frac{a-b}{a+b} \times 450{/tex} = 150 mm
{tex}\therefore \ K_{p}=\frac{150 \times 150}{150}{/tex} = 150

questions.question:
{tex}\Delta G_{f}^{\circ}{/tex} for N2O4 and NO2 are 23.49 and 12.39 kcal at 27°C. The equilibrium constant Kp for the given reaction at 27°C is:
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g){/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta G^{\circ}{/tex} for the change {tex}=2 \times \Delta G_{\mathrm{NO}_{2}}^{\circ},-\Delta G_{\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}}^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \times 12.39-23.49{/tex}
= 1.29 kcal
Now {tex}\Delta G^{\circ}{/tex} = -2.303 RT log Kp
{tex}1.29 \times 10^{3}=-2.303 \times 2 \times 300 \log K_{p}{/tex}
log Kp = 0.94
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Kp = 0.1148

questions.question:
Kc for the reaction:
[Ag(CN)2]- {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} Ag+ + 2CN-, the equilibrium constant at 25°C is 4.0 {tex}\times{/tex}10-19 then the silver ion concentration in a solution which was originally 0.1 molar in KCN and 0.03 molar in AgNO3 is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {2KCN}\limits_{0.1} }\limits_{(0.1 - 0.06)} + \mathop {\mathop {AgN{O_3}}\limits_{0.03} }\limits_0 \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {Ag(CN)_2^ - }\limits_0 }\limits_{0.03} + \mathop {\mathop {KN{O_3}}\limits_0 }\limits_{0.03} + \mathop {\mathop {{K^ + }}\limits_0 }\limits_{0.03} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad\left[\mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{CN})_{2}^{-}\right]=0.03{/tex}
Now {tex}\begin{array}{ccc} \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{CN})_{2}^{-} & \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ag}^{+}+ & 2 \mathrm{CN}^{-} \\ 0.03 & 0 & 0.04 \\ (0.03-a) & a & (0.04+a) \end{array}{/tex} (left from KCN)
KC is small
{tex}\therefore \ 0.03-a \approx 0.03{/tex} and {tex}0.04+a \approx 0.04{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ K_{c}=4 \times 10^{-19}=\frac{(0.04)^{2} \times a}{0.03}{/tex}; {tex}\therefore a=7.5 \times 10^{-18}{/tex}

questions.question:
For a given reaction:
[Cu(NH3)4]2+ + H2O {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} [Cu(NH3)3 • H2O]2+ + NH3 net rate of reaction is = 2.0 {tex}\times{/tex}10-4s-1[Cu(NH3)4]2+ [H2O] - 3.0 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 L mol-1 s-1 [Cu(NH3)3H2O]2+ [NH3]. What is the value of equilibrium constant for the given reaction?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{f}=2.0 \times 10^{-4}{/tex}
{tex}K_{b}=3.0 \times 10^{5}{/tex}
Thus, {tex}K_{\mathrm{eq.}}=\frac{K_{f}}{K_{b}}=\frac{2.0 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}}{3.0 \times 10^{5} \text { litre } \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}}{/tex}
{tex}=0.66 \times 10^{-9}{/tex} mol litre-1

questions.question: In the reaction C(s) + CO2 (g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2CO(g), the equilibrium pressure is 12 atm. If 50% of CO2 reacts, Kp for the change is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total moles = 1.5 and {tex}\Delta n=1{/tex}
Total pressure given equilibrium = 12 atm
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{\left(n_{\mathrm{CO}}\right)^{2}}{\left(n_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}\right)} \times\left[\frac{P}{\Sigma n}\right]^{\Delta n}=\frac{(1)^{2}}{0.5} \times\left(\frac{12}{1.5}\right)^{1}{/tex}
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{12}{1.5 \times 0.5}{/tex} = 16 atm

questions.question: When 20 g of CaCO3 were put into 10 litre flask and heated to 800°C, 35% of CaCO3 remained unreacted at equilibrium. Kp for decomposition of CaCO3 is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Moles of CO2 formed {tex}=\frac{20 \times 65}{10^{4}}=1.3 \times 10^{-1}{/tex}
{tex}\because \quad P V=n R T{/tex}
{tex}p_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}=\frac{1.3 \times 10^{-1}}{10}{/tex}{tex}\times 0.0821 \times 1073{/tex} = 1.145 atm
Now {tex}K_{p}=p_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}^{\prime}{/tex} = 1.145 atm

questions.question: For the reaction N2(g) + O2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO(g), the equilibrium constant is K1. The equilibrium constant is K2 for the reaction 2NO(g) + O2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO2(g). What is K for the reaction NO2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex}{tex}\frac {1}{ 2} N _{2(g)}+ O _{2(g)} ?{/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
So, for the reverse reaction which is the desired one the value of K will be reciprocal of this value. i.e.
{tex}K =\frac{1}{\left( K _1 K _2\right)^\frac {1 }{ 2}}{/tex}

questions.question:
The dissociation equilibrium of a gas AB2 can be represented as:
2AB2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2AB(g) + B2(g)
The degree of dissociation is x and is small compared to 1. The expression relating the degree of dissociation (x) with equilibrium constant Kp and total pressure P is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {2A{B_2}}\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - x} (g) \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2AB(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_x + \mathop {\mathop {{B_2}(g)}\limits_0 }\limits_\frac {x}{2} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore K_{P}=\frac{x^{2} \cdot x}{2(1-x)^{2}} \cdot\left[\frac{P}{1+\frac{x}{2}}\right]^{1}=\frac{x^{3} \cdot P}{2}{/tex} (1 - x {tex}\approx{/tex} 1 and 1 + {tex}\frac{x}{2}={/tex} 1, since c << 1)
or {tex}x=\sqrt[3]{\frac{2 K_{P}}{P}}{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following lines correctly show the temperature dependence of equilibrium constant K, for an exothermic reaction?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\because \ K=A e^{-\frac{\Delta H}{R T}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ \ln K=\ln A-\frac{\Delta H}{R} \times \frac{1}{T}{/tex}
Slope {tex}=-\frac{\Delta H}{R}{/tex}
Since {tex}\Delta H{/tex} -ve and thus slope is positive. Positive slope appears in lines represented by A and B.

questions.question:
A reversible reaction:
 is initiated with x mole of A and y mole of B. At equilibrium a mole of A are consumed than a is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop A\limits_x }\limits_{(x - a)} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop B\limits_y }\limits_{(y + a)} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{K_{1}}{K_{2}}=\frac{y+a}{x-a}{/tex}
or K2y + K2a = K1x - K1a
or {tex}a=\frac{K_{1} x-K_{2} y}{K_{1}+K_{2}}{/tex}

questions.question:
Conversion of A to B occurs simultaneously by paths

  2.  (H+ is catalyst)

The correction relation is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{K_{1}}{K_{2}}=\frac{[B]}{[A]}{/tex}
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{K_{3}}{K_{4}}=\frac{[B]\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]}{[A]\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]}=\frac{[B]}{[A]}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ \frac{K_{1}}{K_{2}}=\frac{K_{3}}{K_{4}}{/tex}
or K1K4 = K2K3

questions.question:

At 1073K and 1 atm, the equilibrium mixture effuses 1.6 times as fast as SO2 under similar conditions. Calculate Kp in atm.

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

NH4Cl(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} NH3(g) + HCl(g)?

PCl5, PCl3 and Cl2 are at equilibrium at 500 K in a closed container and their concentrations are 0.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1, 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L1 and 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1 respectively at equilibrium.
The value of Kc for the reaction,
PCl5(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} PCl3(g) + Cl2(g) will be:

One mole of SO3 was placed in one litre vessel to attain the equilibrium at T K.

SO3 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} SO2 + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}O2?

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants for the reactions are:
H3PO4 {tex}\stackrel{K_{\mathrm{1}}}{\rightleftharpoons}{/tex} H+ + H2{tex}PO_4^-{/tex}; K1
H2P{tex}O_4^-{/tex} {tex}\stackrel{K_{\mathrm{2}}}{\rightleftharpoons}{/tex} H+ + H2{tex}PO_4^{2-}{/tex}; K2
H3P{tex}O_4^{2-}{/tex} {tex}\stackrel{K_{\mathrm{3}}}{\rightleftharpoons}{/tex} H+ + H2{tex}PO_4^{3-}{/tex}; K3
The equilibrium constant for, H3PO4 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 3H+ + {tex}PO_4^{3-}{/tex} will be:

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: 0.61 atm-1
