questions.question: The correct relation between Kp and Kc for the reaction aX + bY {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} bZ + aW is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The relationship between Kp​ and Kc​ is Kp ​= Kc​(RT){tex}\Delta{/tex}n
For the reaction aX + bY {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} bZ + aW, the value of {tex}\Delta{/tex}n is b + a − (a + b) = 0.
Substitute this value in the above expression.
Kp = Kc(RT) {tex}\Delta{/tex}n = Kc​(RT)0 = Kc

questions.question:
The values of {tex}K_{p_1}{/tex} and {tex}K_{p_2}{/tex} for the reactions:

  1. X {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Y + Z
  2. A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B

are in the ratio 9 : 1. Assuming degree of dissociation of X and A be same, the dissociation pressure at equilibrium (i) and (ii) are in the ratio:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question:
For the reaction in equilibrium,
N2O4(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g)
the concentrations, of N2O4 and NO2 at equilibrium are 4.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 and 1.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 mol L-1 respectively. The value of Kc for this reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The equilibrium constant for the given reaction is-
Kc = {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {{\text{N}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{2}}}} \right]}^{\text{2}}}}}{{\left[ {{{\text{N}}_{\text{2}}}{{\text{O}}_{\text{4}}}} \right]}}{/tex}
Kc = {tex}\frac{\left(1.2 \ \times \ 10^{-2} \mathrm{~mol} / \mathrm{L}\right)^{2}}{4.8 \ \times \ 10^{-2} \mathrm{~mol} / \mathrm{L}}{/tex}
Kc = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 mol L-1

questions.question: 1.1 mole of A are mixed with 2.2 mole of B and the mixture is then kept in one litre flask till the equilibrium is attained A + 2B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2C + D. At the equilibrium, 0.2 mole of C are formed. The equilibrium constant of the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\underset{aa - x}A + \underset{bb - 2x}{2B} \rightleftharpoons \underset{02x}{2C} + \underset{0x}D{/tex}
Given, 2x = 0.2, Also, a = 1.1
x = 0.1b = 2.2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Kc​ = {tex}\frac{4 x^{3}}{(a \ - \ x)(b \ - \ 2 x)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4 \ \times \ (0.1)^{3}}{(1.1 \ - \ 0.1)(2.2 \ - \ 0.2)^{2}}{/tex} = 0.001

questions.question: The equilibrium constant for the reaction; P4(s) + 5O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} P4O10(s) is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The equilibrium reaction is P4(s) + 5O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} P4O10(s)
The expression for the equilibrium reaction is Kc ​= {tex}\frac{\left[{P}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{10}\right]}{\left[{P}_{4}\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{5}}=\frac{1}{1 \ \times \ \left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{5}}{/tex}
Here, [P4​O10​] = [P4​] = 1 as the partial pressures of the solid substances are considered as unity.

questions.question: Eight mole of a gas AB3 attain equilibrium in a closed container of volume 1 dm3 as, 2AB3 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} A2(g) + 3B2(g). If at equilibrium 2 mole of A2 are present constant is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: 27 mol2 L-2

questions.question: The equilibrium constant for the reaction, N2(g) + O2(g) {tex}\leftrightharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g) is 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 at 2000 K. In presence of a catalyst the equilibrium is attained 10 times faster. Therefore, the equilibrium constant, in presence of the catalyst, at 2000 K is:

options.option: difficult to compute without more data

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Therefore the equilibrium constant in the presence of a catalyst at 2000 K is same as the equilibrium constant in the absence of catalyst at 2000 K. It is 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4.
The presence of catalyst does not affect the value of the equilibrium constant. It only increases the rates of forward and reverse reaction to equal extent.

questions.question: The reaction, A + 2B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2C + D was studied using an initial concentration of B which was 1.5 times that of A. But the equilibrium concentrations of A and C were found to be equal. Then the Kc for the equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
A + 2B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2C + D
At t = 0 {tex}\alpha \quad \frac{\mathbf{3}}{{2}} {\alpha}{/tex} 0 0
At eq. {tex}\alpha-x \frac{3}{2} \alpha{/tex} - x 2x x
Given, [A] = [C]
a - x = 2x
{tex}\therefore \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{3}{/tex}
Now, Kc = {tex}\frac {{[{C}]^{2}[{D}]}}{[{A}][{B}]^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{2 a}{3}\right)^{2} \times \frac{a}{3}}{\left(a-\frac{a}{3}\right)\left(\frac{3 a}{2}-\frac{2 a}{3}\right)^{2}}{/tex} = 0.32

questions.question:
Given, HF + H2O {tex}\xrightarrow{{{K_a}}}{/tex} H3O+ + F-
F- + H2O {tex}\xrightarrow{{{K_b}}}{/tex} HF + OH-
Which relation is correct?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
HF + H2O {tex}\stackrel{{K}_{\mathrm{a}}}{\longrightarrow}{/tex} H3O+ + F-
F- + H2O {tex}\stackrel{{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}{\longrightarrow}{/tex} HF + OH- 
When the above two reactions are added, the net reaction is
2H2O {tex}\stackrel{{K}_{\mathrm{b}}}{\longrightarrow}{/tex} H3O+ + OH-
Ka = {tex}\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}_{3} \mathrm{O}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]}{[\mathrm{HF}]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]}{/tex} and Kb = {tex}\frac{[\mathrm{HF}]\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Ka {tex}\times{/tex} Kb = [H3O+] [OH-] = Kw
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Ka  {tex}\times{/tex} Kb = Kw

questions.question: {tex}\Delta G^{\circ}{/tex} for the reaction X + Y {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} Z is -4.606 kcal. The equilibrium constant for the reaction at 227oC is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question: The partial pressure of CH3OH(g), CO(g) and H2(g) in equilibrium mixture for the reaction, CO(g) + 2H2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} CH3OH(g) are 2.0, 1.0 and 0.1 atm respectively at 427°C. The value of Kp for the decomposition of CH3OH to CO and H2 is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{p_{\mathrm{CH}, \mathrm{OH}}}{p_{\mathrm{CO}} \ \times \ p_{\mathrm{H}_{2}}^{2}}=\frac{2}{1 \ \times \ (0.1)^{2}}{/tex} = 200
kp for reverse reaction is
= {tex}\frac{1}{K_{p}}=\frac{1}{200}{/tex} = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 atm2

questions.question: For the reaction, C(s) + CO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2CO(g), the partial pressures of CO2 and CO are 2.0 and 4.0 atm respectively at equilibrium. The Kp for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For the reaction, C(s) + CO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2CO(g), the partial pressure of CO2​ and CO are 2.0 and 4.0 atm, respectively, at equilibrium.
Kp ​= {tex}\frac{{P}_{\mathrm{CO}}^{2}}{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{CO}_2}}{/tex}
Kp ​= {tex}\frac {(4.0)^2​}{2.0}{/tex}
Kp ​= 8.0
Thus, the Kp​ of the reaction is 8.0.

questions.question: In the reaction, PCl5 {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} PCl3 + Cl2, the amounts of PCl5, PCl3 and Cl2 at equilibrium are 2 mole each and the total pressure is 3 atm. The equilibrium constant Kp is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{p}=\frac{{ }^{n} \mathrm{Cl}_{2} \ \times \ { }^{n} \mathrm{PCl}_{3}}{{ }^{n} \mathrm{PCl}_{5}} \times\left[\frac{p}{\Sigma n}\right]^{1}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \times \frac{2}{2} \times\left[\frac{3}{6}\right]^{1}{/tex}
= 1.0 atm

questions.question: For the reaction, CO(g) + Cl2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} COCl2(g) the value of {tex}\frac {K_p}{K_c}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
CO(g) + Cl2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} COCl2(g)
In given equation {tex}\Delta{/tex}n = 1 − (1 + 1) = −1
Relation between Kp and Kc is
Kp ​= Kc​(RT){tex}\Delta{/tex}n
{tex}\frac {​​​​​​​K_p​}{K_c}{/tex}​ = (RT)−1 = {tex}\frac{1}{R T}{/tex}

questions.question: A definite mass of solid NH4HS is placed in a flask already containing ammonia gas at a certain temperature and 0.50 atm pressure. NH4HS decomposes to give NH3 and H2S and at equilibrium total pressure in flask is 0.84 atm. The equilibrium constant for the reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\begin{array}{cccc} & \mathrm{NH}_{4} \mathrm{HS}(s) & \rightleftharpoons & \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g)+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}(g) \\ \mathrm{at} & t=0 & & 0.5 & 0 \\ \mathrm{at} & t=\mathrm{eq} . & & 0.5+P & P \end{array}{/tex}
PT = 0.5 + P + P = 0.84
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = 0.17 atm
Kp = {tex}P_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}} \times P_{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{~S}}{/tex} = (0.5 + 0.17) {tex}\times{/tex} 0.17
= 0.11 atm

questions.question: For an equilibrium change involving gaseous phase, the forward reaction is first order while the reverse reaction is second order. The unit of Kp for the forward equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
r1 = kf [Reactant]
r2 = kb [Product]
{tex}\because{/tex}  r1 = r2
{tex}\therefore K_{c}=\frac{k_{f}}{k_{b}}=\frac{[\text { Product }]^{2}}{[\text { Reactant }]}{/tex}
{tex}\because {/tex} P {tex}\propto{/tex} concentration
Thus, unit will be unit of P i.e., atm.

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants for the disproportionation of HgCl2 into HgCl+ and {tex}HgCl_3^-{/tex} is ________
Given, {tex}\mathrm{HgCl}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{HgCl}_{2}{/tex}; {tex}K_{1}=3 \times 10^{6}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{HgCl}_{2}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{HgCl}_{3}^{-}{/tex}; K2 = 9.0
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6

questions.question: For N2 + 3H2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3 one mole of N2 and three mol of H2 are at pressure of 4 atm. Equilibrium pressure is found to be 3 atm. Hence, Kp is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
N2 + 3H2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3
Initial pressure 1, 3, 0
Final pressure (1 - x), (3 - 3x), 2x
P {tex}\propto{/tex} no. of moles 
{tex}\because{/tex} 1 - x + 3 - 3x + 2x = 3
{tex}\therefore \mathrm{x}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 0.5
{tex}\therefore {P}_{{N}_{2}}{/tex} = 0.5, {tex}{P}_{{H}_{2}}{/tex} = 1.5, {tex}P_{NH_3}{/tex} = 1.0
Thus, Kp = {tex}\frac{1}{(0.5) \ \times \ (1.5)^{3}}{/tex}

questions.question:
Consider a hypothetical equilibrium:
A(aq.) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B(aq.) + C(aq.)
Before the reaction, A and B were present such that mole fraction of B is {tex}\frac 13{/tex} as well as molar concentration of B is M. At equilibrium the reaction mixture equal to {tex}\frac 12{/tex}. The equilibrium constant of the reaction can be given by:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{9 M^{2}}{28}{/tex}

questions.question:
One mole of A at pressure P in a closed container of 1 litre shows the following equilibria:
A(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} B(g) + C(g); K1
C(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} D(g) + B(g) K2
The pressure at equilibrium is 2P and {tex}\frac{[C]_{e q}}{[A]_{e q}}{/tex}= 2.1. The ratio of {tex}\frac{K_{1}}{K_{2}}{/tex} can be given by:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question: For the given endothermic equilibrium, the heat of reaction at constant volume is 1200 cal more than that of heat of reaction at constant pressure at 300 K. The ratio of equilibrium constants Kp and Kc for the above equilibrium is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Given that: {tex}\Delta{/tex}U − {tex}\Delta{/tex}H = 1200cal
where {tex}\Delta{/tex}U is the heat of reaction at constant volume and {tex}\Delta{/tex}H is the heat of reaction at constant pressure.
Also, we have {tex}\Delta{/tex}H = {tex}\Delta{/tex}U + {tex}\Delta{/tex}nRT
{tex}\therefore \ \Delta{/tex}nRT = −1200
or {tex}\Delta{/tex}n = {tex}\frac{-1200}{2 \ \times \ 300}{/tex} = −2 (R = 2cal)
Now, Kp ​= Kc​(RT){tex}\Delta{/tex}n
{tex}\frac{{{{\text{K}}_{\text{p}}}}}{{{{\text{K}}_{\text{c}}}}}{/tex} ​​= (0.0821 {tex}\times{/tex} 300)-2 = 1.64 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3

questions.question: The concentrations of A and B are 3M and 4M respectively in the state of equilibrium of A(g) + 2B(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex}C(g). After the equilibrium is attained, the volume of container is doubled so that equilibrium is restablished and at this state [B]eq is 3M. The equilibrium constant KC for the reaction is:

questions.question: Equal volumes of 0.06 M AgNO3 and 0.2 M KCN solutions are mixed. Kc for the reaction Ag(CN)2(aq.) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Ag+(aq.) + 2CN-(aq.) is 1.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-19 at 25°C. The concentration of Ag+ (aq.) in solution is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question:

The pressure over excess solids is 40 atm and over excess C is 80 atm. The total pressure of the gaseous mixture over the solid mixture is:

questions.question: For the equilibrium: A(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B(g) + 3C(g), the concentration of B and C are x and y mole litre-1 respectively at equilibrium. If concentration of C is two times of y at equilibrium then what would be the value of [B] at equilibrium?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question: 500 mL vessel contains 1.5 Meach of A, B, C and D at equilibrium. If 0.5 Meach of C and D are taken out, the value of KC for A + B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C + D is:

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question: The equilibrium attained in a closed container is H2O(l) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H2O(v) at 40°C. The aqueous tension of water in container is 23 mm at 40°C. What is its Kp?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

questions.question: When 20 g of CaCO3 were put into 10 litre flask and heated to 800°C, 35% of CaCO3 remained unreacted at equilibrium. Calculate Kp for decomposition of CaCO3.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: 1.145 atm

questions.question: A mixture of N2 and H2 in the volume ratio 1 : 5 is allowed to equilibriate at 700 K and 103 atm. Calculate Kp if 0.4 mole fraction of NH3 is formed at equilibrium.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: 2.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 atm-2

questions.question:
Following gas phase transformation equilibria attain simultaneously in a closed vessel.
A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} B; {tex}K_{C_1}{/tex} ...(i)
A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C; {tex}K_{C_2}{/tex} ...(ii)
B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C; {tex}K_{C_3}{/tex} ...(iii)
Let {tex}\alpha_{1}, \alpha_{2}{/tex} and {tex}\alpha_3{/tex} mole of A and B are changed to attain the simultaneous equilibrium (i), (ii), (iii) respectively at constant V and temperature. Then:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: {tex}K_{C_{3}}=\frac{K_{C_{2}}}{K_{C_{1}}}{/tex}

questions.question: The graph relates In Keq vs.{tex}\frac 1T{/tex} for a reaction. The reaction must be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The graph relates ln Keq.​vs.{tex}\frac 1T{/tex}​ for a reaction. The reaction must be exothermic. Adding temperature would cause the reactant concentrations to increase; thus, equilibrium constant K will decrease.
In {tex}\frac{\mathrm{K}_{2}}{\mathrm{~K}_{1}}=\frac{\Delta \mathrm{H}}{\mathrm{R}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{T}_{2} \ - \ \mathrm{T}_{1}}{\mathrm{~T}_{1} \mathrm{~T}_{2}}\right]{/tex}
Since, K increases with decrease of temperature and thus, {tex}\Delta{/tex}H = −ve.
An exothermic reaction occurs when the temperature of a system increases due to the evolution of heat. This heat is released into the surroundings, resulting in an overall negative quantity for the heat of reaction. A system that releases heat to the surroundings, an exothermic reaction, has a negative {tex}\Delta{/tex}H by convention, because the enthalpy of the products is lower than the enthalpy of the reactants of the system.

questions.question: Which of the following variation represents endothermic nature?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
ln Kp ​= {tex}\frac{\Delta \mathrm{H}}{{R T}^{2}}{/tex}
Kp ​= {tex}\mathrm{e}^{-\frac{\Delta {H}}{{R T}}}{/tex}
Kp increases exponentially with temperature for endothermic reactions.
A system of reactants that absorbs heat from the surroundings in an endothermic reaction has a positive {tex}\Delta H{/tex} because the enthalpy of the products is higher than the enthalpy of the reactants of the system.
If Kp​ increases with an increase in temperature, the reaction to shifts to the right.

questions.question: In an experiment to determine the enthalpy of neutralization of sodium hydroxide with sulphuric acid, 50 cm3 of 0.4 M sodium hydroxide were titrated thermometrically with 0.25 M sulphuric acid. Which of the following plots gives the correct representation?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Meq. of H2SO4 ​needed for 20 Meq. of NaOH = 20
Thus, a volume of H2SO4 ​needed = VmL
or V {tex}\times{/tex} normality = 20
V {tex}\times{/tex} 0.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 20
{tex}\therefore{/tex} V = 40mL
Also, temperature increases during neutralization and then decreases due to the increase in the volume of the solution.

questions.question:
For the reaction:
Fe2N(s) + {tex}\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{~g}){/tex}  {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2 Fe(s) + NH3(g)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}K_{\mathrm{p}}=K_{\mathrm{c}}(\mathrm{RT})^{\Delta \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{g}}}{/tex} = {tex}K_{\mathrm{c}}(\mathrm{RT})^{1-\frac 3 2}=K_{\mathrm{c}}(\mathrm{RT})^{-\frac 1 2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Kc = {tex}K_{\mathrm{p}}(\mathrm{RT})^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}

questions.question: If the equilibrium constant for {tex}\mathrm{A} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} is {tex}\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{eq}}^{(1)}{/tex} and that of B + C {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} P is {tex}\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{eq}}^{(2)}{/tex}, the equilibrium constant for {tex}\mathrm{A} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{P}{/tex} is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathrm{A} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{B}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}; {tex}K_{\mathrm{eq}}^{(1)}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\mathrm{B}+\mathrm{C} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{P}{/tex}; {tex}K_{\mathrm{eq}}^{(2)}{/tex} ...(ii)
On adding equations (i) and (ii), we get
A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} P
Keq (overall) = {tex}K_{\mathrm{eq}}^{(1)} \cdot K_{\mathrm{eq}}^{(2)}{/tex}

questions.question: A solid XY kept in an evacuated sealed container undergoes decomposition to form a mixture of gases X and Y at temperature T. The equilibrium pressure is 10 bar in the vessel. Kp for this reaction is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\text{XY}}({\text{s}}) \rightleftharpoons \mathop {{\text{X}}({\text{g}})}\limits_p + \mathop {{\text{Y}}({\text{g}})}\limits_p {/tex}
At eqm., Total pressure = 2p = 10 bar
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = 5; Now Kp = (px) (py) = p2 = 25

questions.question: In the figure shown below reactant A (represented by square) is in equilibrium with product B (represented by circle). The equilibrium constant is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Equilibrium constant
{tex}K_{c}=\frac{[\mathrm{B}]}{[\mathrm{A}]}=\frac{11}{6} \approx 2{/tex}

questions.question:
Calculate the value of equilibrium constant for the reaction X {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Y if:
X {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} A; K1 = 4
A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} B; K2 = 2
B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} B; K3 = 6
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
The required equation is obtained by adding the three given equations.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Y, K = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 48

questions.question: Write pressure in terms of concentration and temperature.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
P = CRT
We all know that the ideal gas equation is
PV = nRT;
P = nRT/V;
P = CRT here N/v is concentration, P is the pressure, V is the volume, n is the number of moles, C is the concentration, R is the universal gas constant and T is the temperature.

questions.question:
Given the following reactions, find K3.

  1. N2 + O2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO ... K1 = 4.3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-25
  2. 2NO + O2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2 ... K2 = 6.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 109
  3. 2NO + 2NO2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 3O2 + 2N2 K3 = ?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Reverse (i): 2NO2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2 + O2 ... (i)
K1 = 2.32 {tex}\times{/tex} 1024
Reverse (ii): 2NO2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO + O2 ... (ii)
K2 = 1.56 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-10
Multiply (i) by 2,
4NO {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2N2 + 2O2 ... (iii)
K1 = 5.38 {tex}\times{/tex} 1048
Add (ii + iii)
2NO2 + 2NO {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 3O2 + 2N2
K3 = 8.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1038

questions.question: In the reversible reaction A + B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C + D, the concentration of each C and D at equilibrium is 0.4 mol L-1 and concentration of each A and B is 0.6 mol L-1, then the equilibrium constant K will be ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Balanced equation: A + B {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C + D
Equilibrium constant, K = {tex}\frac{[{C}][{D}]}{[{A}][{B}]}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{0.4 \times 0.4}{0.6 \times 0.6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex}

questions.question:
For the reaction:
N2(g) + 3H2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3(g) at 472oC
Kp = 2.79 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5
Calculate the value of Kc.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Kp = 2.79 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5
Kp = Kc(RT){tex}\triangle n{/tex}
{tex}\triangle{/tex}n = 2 - 4 = -2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (2.79 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5) = Kc(0.0821 {tex}\times{/tex} 745)-2
Kc = 1.04 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-1

questions.question:
At certain temperature, the equilibrium constant (Kc) is 4 for the reaction: SO2(g) + NO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} SO3(g) + NO(g)
If we take one mole of each of the four gases in one-liter container, what will be the equilibrium concentration of NO?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Chemical equation is:

  SO2(g) + NO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} SO3(g ) + NO(g)
Initial (M) 1 1 1 1
Change (M) -x -x +x +x
Equilibrium (M) 1 - x 1 - x 1 + x 1 + x

Kc = {tex}\frac{\left[{SO}_{3}\right][{NO}]}{\left[{SO}_{2}\right]\left[{NO}_{2}\right]}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 4 = {tex}\frac{(1+x)(1+x)}{(1-x)(1-x)}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 4 = {tex}\frac{(1+x)^{2}}{(1-x)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2 = {tex}\frac{1+x}{1-x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1 + x = 2 - 2x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3x = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} = 0.33
{tex}\therefore{/tex} [NO] = 1 + x = 1 + 0.33 = 1.33 M

questions.question:
For the following reaction, Kc = 1.3 {tex}\times{/tex} 1022, at 25oC.
CH4(g) + H2O(g)  CO(g) + 3H2(g)
Find the value of Kp at the same temperature for the reaction,
{tex}\frac{1}{2} {CO}_{({g})}+\frac{3}{2} {H}_{2({g})} \rightleftharpoons \frac{1}{2} {CH}_{4({g})}+\frac{1}{2} {H}_{2} {O}_{({g})}{/tex}
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
CH4 + H2O {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} CO + 3H2 Kc = 1.3 {tex}\times{/tex} 1022
Reverse the reaction.
CO + 3H2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} CH4 + H2O Kc = 7.7 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-23
Divide the above reaction by 2.
{tex}\frac{1}{2} {CO}_{({g})}+\frac{3}{2} {H}_{2({g})} \rightleftharpoons \frac{1}{2} {CH}_{4({g})}+\frac{1}{2} {H}_{2} {O}_{({g})}{/tex}
{tex}K_c^"{/tex} = 8.7 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-12
Kp = {tex}K_c^"(RT)^{\triangle n}{/tex} = (8.7 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-12) (0.082 {tex}\times{/tex} 298)-1
Kp = 3.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-13

questions.question:
A 20 L flask at 500 K initially contains AB(g) at a pressure of 4 atm. At equilibrium, if the partial pressure of A2(g) is 0.387 atm, find the equilibrium partial pressure of AB.
2AB(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} A2(g) + B2(g)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
2AB {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} A2 + B2

atm AB A2 B2
I 4 0 0
C -2x +x +x
E (4 - 2x) 0.387 0.387

pAB = 4 - 2x = 4 - 2(0.387) = 3.23 atm

questions.question:
Consider the decomposition of ammonium chloride. If a 1L flask is charged with 4.1 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-1 moles of the ammonium chloride and at equilibrium, the flask contains 2.3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 moles of ammonia, how many moles of HCl are present at equilibrium?
Given Kc = 1.58 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-8
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
NH4Cl(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} NH3(g) + HCl(g)
Kc = [NH3][HCl]
1.58 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-8 = [2.3 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3][HCl]
[HCl] = 6.9 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6 
Moles of HCl = 6.9 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6

questions.question: Solid ammonium carbamate (NH2COONH4) decomposes to give ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas. If we start with 2 moles of the compound and the total pressure at equilibrium is 5.81 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 atm, find Kp.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
NH2COONH4(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3(g) + CO2(g)
Kp = [NH3]2 [CO2]
Kp = (2x)2(x) = 4x3
Total pressure = 2x + x = 3x = 5.81 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 0.0193
Kp = 4(0.0193)3
Kp = 2.9 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5

questions.question: At 25oC, equilibrium is established in the reversible reaction 2A(g) + B(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2C(g). The equilibrium concentrations are [A] = 0.5 M, [B] = 0.3 M, [C] = 0.4 M. What is the value of Kp for this reaction at 25oC?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Kc = {tex}\frac{[0.4]^{2}}{[0.3][0.5]^{2}}{/tex} = 2.13
Kp = Kc(RT){tex}\triangle n{/tex} = 2.13(0.0821 {tex}\times{/tex} 298)-1 = 0.087

questions.question:
Equilibrium constant for the reaction represented below is 50 at 448oC:
H2(g) + I2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2HI(g)
When 1.0 mole of H2 is mixed with 1.0 mole of I2 in a 0.50 L flask and allowed to react, at the end of the reaction, how many moles of I2 are left unreacted? [Given: {tex}\sqrt{50}{/tex} = 7.07]
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
H2(g) + I2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2HI(g)

(M) H2 I2 HI
I 2 2 0
C -x -x +2x
E (2 - x) (2 - x) 2x

Kc = {tex}\frac{[{HI}]^{2}}{\left[{H}_{2}\right]\left[{I}_{2}\right]}{/tex}
Kc = {tex}\frac{(2 x)^{2}}{(2-x)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt{50}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x}{2-x}{/tex}
x = 1.56
[I2]eq = 2 - 1.56 = 0.44 M = 0.22 moles
Moles left unreacted = Moles at equilibrium = 0.22 moles

questions.question: Consider a reaction carried out at two different temperatures T1 and T2 (T2 > T1) with equilibrium constants K1 and K2, respectively. If the reaction is endothermic, then the relation between K1 and K2 is ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: K2 > K1

questions.question:
Consider the following reaction:
C2H6(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C2H4(g) + H2(g)
If the initial pressure is 0.67 atm and the total pressure at equilibrium is 0.92 atm, calculate Kc for the reaction at 298 K.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
atm C2H6 C2H4 H2
I 0.67 0 0
C -x +x +x
E (0.67 - x) x x

0.67 - x + x + x = 0.92
x = 0.25
Kp = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{(0.67-x)}{/tex}
Kp = 0.148
Kp = Kc(RT){tex}\triangle n{/tex}
Kc = {tex}\frac{0.148}{(0.0821 \times 298)^1}{/tex}
Kc = 6.05 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3

questions.question:
Identify the CORRECT equilibrium expression for the following reaction.
MgO(s) + CO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} MgCO3(S)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: In heterogeneous equilibria, the concentration of pure solids and pure liquids are not included in the equilibrium expression.

questions.question:
Identify the CORRECT balanced reaction that corresponds to the following equilibrium expression.
[HCl]4[O2] Kc = [H2O]2 [Cl2]2
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: 4HCl(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2H2O(g) + 2Cl2(g)

questions.question: For the reaction HI(g) + I2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} HI3(g) at 25oC:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\triangle n{/tex} = -1
Kp = Kc(RT)-1
{tex}\frac{{K}_{{e}}}{{K}_{{c}}}=\frac{1}{({RT})}=\frac{{I}}{(0.0821 \times 298)}{/tex} = 0.04

RT 24.46
{tex}\sqrt{RT}{/tex} 4.94
{tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{RT}}{/tex} 0.20

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{K_p}{K_c} < \frac{1}{\sqrt{{RT}}}{/tex}

questions.question:
Consider the following equilibrium:
{tex}\frac{1}{2} {A}_{2} {B}_{4({g})} \rightleftharpoons {AB}_{2({~g})}{/tex} Kc = 3.3 at 100oC
For which of the following equilibria is Kc less than 3.3 at 100oC?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
(A) Kc = 10.9 ; (B) Kc = 1.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 104
(C) Kc = 1.82 ; (D) Kc= 118.6

questions.question: A 0.50 mol sample of N2O4(g) is allowed to come to equilibrium with NO2(g) in a 0.750 L flask. At equilibrium, 0.42 mol N2O4 remains. Calculate equilibrium constant Kc for the decomposition of N2O4.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
N2O4(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g)
M N2O4 NO2
I 0.66 0
C -0.10 +0.20
E 0.56 0.20

Kc = {tex}\frac{\left[{NO}_{2}\right]^{2}}{\left[{N}_{2} {O}_{4}\right]}=\frac{(0.2)^{2}}{0.56}{/tex} = 0.071

questions.question:
At 450oC, ammonia decomposes according to the following equilibrium equation:

  2NH3(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2(g) + 3H2(g)
(M) NH3 N2 H2
Initial 0.255 M 0 0
Change      
Equilibrium     0.342 M

Based on the data provided in the above table, find Kc.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
2NH3(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2(g) + 3H2(g)
(M) NH3 N2 H2
I 0.255 0 0
C 0.228 0.114 0.342
E 0.027 0.114 0.342

Kc = {tex}\frac{(0.114)(0.342)^{3}}{(0.027)^{2}}{/tex} = 6.25

questions.question: If the equilibrium constant for N2(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g) is K, the equilibrium constant for {tex}\frac{1}{2} {N}_{2({g})}+\frac{1}{2} {O}_{2({g})} \rightleftharpoons {NO}_{({g})}{/tex} will be:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For the reaction, N2(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g)
K = {tex}\frac{[{NO}]^{2}}{\left[{N}_{2}\right]\left[{O}_{2}\right]}{/tex}
For the reaction, {tex}\frac{1}{2} {N}_{2({g})}+\frac{1}{2} {O}_{2({g})} \rightleftharpoons {NO}_{({g})}{/tex}
K' = {tex}\frac{[{NO}]}{\left[{N}_{2}\right]^\frac {1 }{ 2}\left[{O}_{2}\right]^\frac {1 }{ 2}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} K' = {tex}K^\frac {1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question:
For the reaction at 298 K,
A(g) + B(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} C(g) + D(g)
{tex}\triangle{/tex}Ho = -29.8 kcal, {tex}\triangle{/tex}So = -0.1 kcal K-1
The value of equilibrium constant is ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\Delta H^{\circ}{/tex} = -29.8 kJ mol-1
{tex}\Delta S^{\circ}{/tex} = -1.00 kJ K-1
From the equation
{tex}\Delta G^{\circ}{/tex} = {tex}\Delta H^{\circ}{/tex} - {tex}T \Delta S^o {/tex} = -29.8 - (298 {tex}\times{/tex} )-0.100))
= -29.8 + 29.8 = 0
Now, {tex}\Delta G^{\circ}{/tex} = -2.303 RTlog Keq
0 = -2.303 RT log Keq {tex}\therefore{/tex} Keq = 1.

questions.question:
The equilibrium constants of the following are:
N2 + 3H2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3; K1
N2 + O2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO; K2
H2 + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}O2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H2O; K3
The equilibrium constant (K) of the reaction:
2NH3 + {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}O2 {tex}\overset{k}{\rightleftharpoons}{/tex} 2NO + 3H2O, will be ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
2NH3 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2 + 3H2; {tex}(\frac{1}{K_1}){/tex}
{tex}\begin{aligned} &3 \mathrm{H}_{2}+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} ;\left(\mathrm{K}_{3}\right)^{3} \\ &\mathrm{~N}_{2}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO} \quad ;\left(\mathrm{K}_{2}\right) \\ &\hline 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}+\frac{5}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2} \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \\ \hline \end{aligned}{/tex}
K = {tex}(\frac{1}{K_1}){/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (K3)3 {tex}\times{/tex} (K2) = {tex}\frac{{K}_{2}\left({~K}_{3}\right)^{3}}{{K}_{{1}}}{/tex}

questions.question: For a reaction at equilibrium, if {tex}\triangle n{/tex} < 0, then ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: Kp < Kc

questions.question:
Consider the following reaction:
PCl5(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} PCl3(g) + Cl2(g)
The reaction reaches a point of equilibrium at which [Cl2] = 0.4 M. The concentration of the reactant at equilibrium is 0.1 M more than its initial concentration, and Kc for the reaction is 0.0454, find the initial concentration of PCl5.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
PCl5(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} PCl3(g) + Cl2(g)
(M) PCl5 PCl3 Cl2
I x - 0.1 0.5 0.5
C +0.1 -0.1 -0.1
E x 0.4 0.4

Kc = {tex}\frac{(0.4)(0.4)}{x}{/tex}
0.0454x = 0.16
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 3.52
x - 0.1 = 3.42
[PCl5]initial = 3.42 M

questions.question: The reaction between N2 and H2 to form ammonia has Kc = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 at the temperature 500oC. The numerical value of Kp for this reaction is ________.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
N2 + 3H2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3
{tex}\triangle n{/tex} = 2 - 4 = -2
500oC = 500 + 273 = 773 K
Kp = Kc(RT){tex}\triangle n{/tex}
Kp = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 {tex}\times{/tex} (0.0821 {tex}\times{/tex} 773)-2
Kp = {tex}\frac{6 \times 10^{-2}}{(0.0821 \times 773)^{2}}{/tex} = 1.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5

questions.question:
For the following reaction, calculate the value of Kp, if Kc = 4.63 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3 at 25oC.
Br2(g) + Cl2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2BrCl(g)
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Kp = Kc(RT){tex}\triangle n{/tex}
= 4.63 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3(0.0821 {tex}\times{/tex} 298)o
= 4.63 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} when {tex}\triangle{/tex}n = 0, Kp = Kc

questions.question:
The following diagrams represent the equilibrium state for three different reactions of the type:
A + X {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} AX (where X = B, C or D)

Each molecule represents a concentration of 1 mol/L. Arrange the reactions in increasing order of their equilibrium constant values.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
K = {tex}\frac{[{AX}]}{[{A}][{X}]}{/tex}

  1. K = 0.5
  2. K = 3
  3. K = 0.11

questions.question:
Figure ‘X’ represents the reaction 3A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B at equilibrium.

Indicate the direction in which the reactions in i, ii and iii will proceed in order to reach equilibrium.

options.option: i - right, ii - left, iii - right
i - left, ii - right, iii - left
i - neither, ii - left, iii - right
i - neither, ii - right, iii - left

questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Equilibrium mixture:
K = {tex}\frac{[{B}]^{2}}{[{A}]^{3}}=\frac{(4)^{2}}{(3)^{3}}{/tex} = 0.59

  1. Q = {tex}\frac{[B]^{2}}{[A]^{3}}=\frac{(3)^{2}}{(3)^{3}}{/tex} = 0.33 < K
  2. Q = {tex}\frac{[B]^{2}}{[A]^{3}}=\frac{(4)^{2}}{(2)^{3}}{/tex} = 2 > K
  3. Q = {tex}\frac{[B]^{2}}{[A]^{3}}=\frac{(3)^{2}}{(5)^{3}}{/tex} = 0.072 < K

questions.question: Diagram (i) represents a system at equilibrium for reaction 2A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B. How many O must be added to diagram (ii) for it also to represent a system at equilibrium as shown in diagram (i)?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
For diagram (i):
K = {tex}\frac{[{B}]^{2}}{[{A}]^{2}}=\frac{(4)^{2}}{(6)^{2}}{/tex} = 0.44
For diagram (ii):
0.44 = {tex}\frac{(\text O^2)}{9^2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} O = 6

questions.question: The figure below represents a mixture of two compounds related by the equilibrium reaction 2A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} B. Which of the following must be TRUE of this mixture if the value of K for the reaction is 0.1?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: Q = {tex}\frac{[{B}]}{[{A}]^{2}}=\frac{8}{(11)^{2}}{/tex} = 0.06

questions.question:
Four glass tubes, each sealed with NO2 gas is placed in four different beakers and allowed to reach a state of equilibrium. The following equilibrium exists between NO2 and N2O4.
2NO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2O4(g); {tex}\triangle{/tex}H = -57.2 kJ/mol
Arrange the tubes according to increasing Kp values.
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Temperature
Ice bath 0oC
Dry ice/acetone -78oC
Water bath 25oC
Boiling water 100oC

Since the reaction is exothermic, increasing the temperature will shift equilibrium position to the left. Thus, the value of Kp decreases with increase in temperature.

questions.question: The equilibrium constants for the reaction, Zn(s) + Cu2+(aq) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Zn2+(aq) + Cu(s) and Cu(s) + 2Ag(aq) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Cu2+(aq) + 2Ag(s) are K1 and K2 respectively. The equilibrium constant for the combined reaction is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
As we are combining the two equations.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Equilibrium constant for combined reaction,
i.e. K = K1 {tex}\times{/tex} K2

questions.question: At 363K, pure water has [H2O+] = 10-6 M. The value of Kw at this temperature will be
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
[H2O+] = [OH-] = 10-6 M
Kw = [ 2O+][OH-]
= 10-6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-6 = 10-12 M

questions.question: In the equilibrium, AB {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} A + B, if the equilibrium concentration of A is double, then equilibrium concentration of B will be
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
AB {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} A + B or {tex}K=\frac{[A][B]}{[A B]}{/tex}
If concentration of A is doubled, the equilibrium concentration of B becomes half to maintain K constant.

questions.question: In which condition, the reaction proceeds in the forward direction?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: When QC < KC , then the reaction proceeds in the forward direction.

questions.question:
Predict which of the following reaction will have appreciable concentration of reactants and products?

  1. Cl2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2Cl(g); KC = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-39
  2. Cl2(g) + 2NO(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NOCl(g); KC = 3.7 {tex}\times{/tex} 108
  3. Cl2(g) + 2NO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g); KC = 1.8
  4. All have appreciable concentration of reactants and products
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation: Cl2(g) + 2NO2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g); Kc = 1.8

questions.question:
For a reaction at equilibrium
A(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} B(g) + {tex}\frac 12{/tex}C(g)
the relation between dissociation constant (K), degree of dissociation ({tex}\alpha{/tex}) and equilibrium pressure (p) is given by:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Equilibrium pressure (p) = Pi{tex}\left(1-\alpha+\alpha+\frac{\alpha}{2}\right){/tex}
p = {tex}P_{\mathrm{i}} \times\left(1+\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} PA = {tex}\left(\frac{1-\alpha}{1+\frac{\alpha}{2}}\right) p{/tex}
PB = {tex}\left(\frac{\alpha}{1+\frac{\alpha}{2}}\right){/tex}p; PC = {tex}\left(\frac{\frac{\alpha}{2}}{1+\frac{\alpha}{2}}\right)p{/tex}
{tex}\therefore K=\frac{P_{\mathrm{C}}^{\frac{1}{2}} \times P_{\mathrm{B}}}{P_{\mathrm{A}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} K {tex}=\frac{\alpha^{\frac{3}{2}} p^{\frac{1}{2}}}{(2+\alpha)^{\frac{1}{2}}(1-\alpha)}{/tex}

questions.question:
1. N2(g) + 3H2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3(g), K1
  2. N2(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g), K2
  3. H2(g)+ {tex}\frac 12{/tex}O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H2O(g), K3,

The equation for the equilibrium constant of the reaction 2NH3(g) + {tex}\frac 52{/tex}O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO(g) + 3H2O(g), (K4) in terms of K1, K2 and K3 is:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
To calculate the value of K4 in the given equation we should apply:
eqn. (ii) + eqn.(iii) {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - eqn. (i)
Hence, K4 = {tex}\frac{K_2 K_3^3}{K_1}{/tex}

questions.question: For a concentrated solution of a weak electrolyte (Keq = equilibrium constant) A2B3 of concentration c, the degree of dissociation {tex}\alpha{/tex} is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}\underset{c(1-\alpha)}{{\mathrm A}_2{\;\mathrm B}_3(\mathrm{aq}.)}\rightleftharpoons\underset{2c\alpha}{2{\;\mathrm A}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})}+\underset{3c\alpha}{3{\;\mathrm B}^{2-}(\mathrm{aq})}{/tex}
Keq = {tex}\frac{\left[\mathrm{A}^{3+}\right]^2\left[\mathrm{~B}^{2-}\right]^3}{\left[\mathrm{~A}_2 \mathrm{~B}_3\right]}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4 \mathrm{c}^2 \alpha^2 \times 27 \mathrm{c}^3 \alpha^3}{\mathrm{c}(1-\alpha)}{/tex}, {tex}\alpha{/tex}<< 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Keq = {tex}\frac{108 \mathrm{c}^5 \alpha^5}{\mathrm{c}}{/tex}, {tex}\alpha{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{eq}}}{108 \mathrm{c}^4}\right)^{\frac{1}{5}}{/tex}

questions.question:
The equilibrium constant for the reversible reaction 2A(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B(g) + C(g) is K1 and for the reaction {tex}\frac 32{/tex}A(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} {tex}\frac 32{/tex}B(g) + {tex}\frac 34{/tex}C(g) is K2 
K1 and K2 are related as:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
2A(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B(g) + C(g) K1 ...(i)
{tex}\frac 32{/tex}A(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} {tex}\frac 32{/tex}B(g) + {tex}\frac 34{/tex}C(g) K2 ...(ii)
eq. (ii) is {tex}\frac 34{/tex} times of eq. (i), hence, K2 = {tex}\left(K_1\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}{/tex}

questions.question: Which one of the following is true for any diprotic acid, H2X?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
{tex}H _2 X \rightleftharpoons HX ^{-}+ H ^{+}, K a_1{/tex}
{tex}HX ^{-} \rightleftharpoons X ^{2-}+ H ^{+}, Ka _2{/tex}
In first reaction, H+ comes from a neutral atom while in second H+ comes from an anion which is difficult as compared to first reaction thus,
Ka1 > Ka2 or Ka2 < Ka1.

questions.question: If {tex}\alpha{/tex} is the fraction of HI dissociated at equilibrium in the reactions, 2HI(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} H2(g) + I2(g) starting with the 2 moles of HI, then the total number of moles of reactants and products at equilibrium are:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant

Total number of moles at equilibrium
= 2 - 2{tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 2

questions.question:
Solid ammonium carbamate dissociated according to the given reaction
NH2COONH4 (s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3(g) + CO2(g)
Total pressure of the gases in equilibrium is 5 atm. Hence Kp
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
NH2COONH4(s) {tex}\leftrightharpoons{/tex} {tex}\underset{2\mathrm P}{2{\mathrm{NH}}_3(\mathrm g)}\;+\;\underset{\mathrm P}{{\mathrm{CO}}_2}(\mathrm g){/tex}
Total pressure = 2P = P = 3P = 5 atm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}\frac 53{/tex} atm
Kp = {tex}\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{CO}_2} \times \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{NH}_3}^2{/tex}
= {tex}\frac 53{/tex}atm {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{2 \times 5}{3} \mathrm{~atm}\right){/tex} = 18.51

questions.question: In the equilibrium SO2Cl2(g) {tex}\leftrightharpoons{/tex} SO2(g) + Cl2(g) at 2000 K and 10 atm pressure, % Cl2 = % SO2 = 40 (by volume) Then
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
SO2Cl2 {tex}\leftrightharpoons{/tex} SO2 + Cl2
KP = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{SO}_2} \times \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{C}_2}}{\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{SO}_2} \mathrm{Cl}_2}{/tex}
Given: {tex}\frac{\text V_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}}{\text V_{\text {total }}}{/tex} = 0.4
or, {tex}\frac{\frac{\mathbf{n}_{\mathrm{Cl}_2} \times \mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{p}}}{\frac{\mathrm{n}_{\text {total }} \times \mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{p}}}{/tex} = 0.4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathbf{n}_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}}{\mathbf{n}_{\text {total }}}{/tex} = 0.4
Hence, {tex}\chi_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}{/tex} = 0.4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{P}_{\text {Cl}_2}{/tex} = {tex}\chi_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Ptotal = 0.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 atm = 4 atm
{tex}\mathrm{P}_{\text {SO}_2}{/tex} = 4 atm
and {tex}\mathrm{P}_{\text {SO}_2}{_{\text {Cl}}}_2{/tex} = 2 atm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Kp = {tex}\frac {4\times 4}{2}{/tex} atm = 8 atm

questions.question: The pKb of dimethylamine and pKa of acetic acid are 3.27 and 4.77 respectively at T (K). The correct option for the pH of dimethylammonium acetate solution is
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
Dimethyl ammonium acetate
((CH3)2 NH COOCH3) is a salt of weak acid and weak base whose pH can be calculated as
{tex}pH =\frac{1}{2}\left(p k_w+p k_a-p k_b\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(14 + 4.77 - 3.27)
pH = 7.75

questions.question: In which of the following equilibrium KC and KP are not equal?
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
As we know, Kp = Kc, {tex}\times{/tex} (RT){tex}\Delta{/tex}ng
So, for reaction having same number of gaseous moles on reactants and products side will have same value of Kc and Kp otherwise their values are different.
For reaction, {tex}2 NO _{(g)} \rightleftharpoons N _{2(g)}+ O _{2(g)}{/tex}
{tex}\Delta{/tex}ng = 2 - 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Kp = Kc {tex}\times{/tex}(RT)0
Kp = Kc
For reaction, {tex}SO _{2(g)}+ NO _{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons SO _{3(g)}+ NO _{(g)}{/tex}
{tex}\Delta{/tex}ng = 2 - 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Kp = Kc
For reaction, {tex}SO _{2(g)}+ I _{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2 HI _{(g)}{/tex}
{tex}\Delta n_g{/tex} = 2 - 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Kp = Kc
For reaction, {tex}2C _{(s)}+ O _{2(g)} \rightleftharpoons 2 CO _{2(g)}{/tex}
{tex}\Delta n_g{/tex} = 2 - 3 = -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Kp = Kc {tex}\times{/tex}(RT)-1
Thus, in this reaction Kp {tex}\ne{/tex} Kc

questions.question: The rate of reaction depends upon the:
questions.topicName: Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant
questions.explanation:
According to law of mass action, the rate of a reaction depends upon the active masses or molar concentration of reactants.
Rate of reaction {tex}\propto{/tex} Concentration of reactants

questions.question: What will be the correct order of vapour pressure of water, acetone and ether At {tex}{\text{3}}0^\circ {\text{C}}{/tex}. Given that among these compounds, water has maximum boiling point and ether has minimum boiling point?
questions.explanation: Greater the boiling point, less is the vapour pressure. Hence, the correct order of vapour pressure will be water < acetone < ether.

questions.question: A mixture of N2 and H2 in the ratio of 1:3 attains equilibrium with NH3 when 50% of the original mixture has reacted. If total pressure at equilibrium is P, then partial pressure of NH3 at equilibrium is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{} }\limits_{Mole\,left} \mathop {\mathop {{N_2}}\limits_1 }\limits_{0.5} + \mathop {\mathop {3{H_2}}\limits_3 }\limits_{1.5} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2N{H_3}}\limits_0 }\limits_{1.0} {/tex}
[{tex}\because{/tex} 50% of 4 mole racts i.e., total 2 mole reacts of 1.3 i.e., {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} mole of N2 and H2 reacts.]
Total mole at equilibrium = 0.5 + 1.5 + 1.0 = 3
{tex}\therefore P_{\mathrm{NH}_{3}}^{\prime}=\frac{1}{3} \times P=\frac{P}{3}{/tex}

questions.question:
Acetic acid tends to form dimer due to hydrogen bonding
 {tex}K_{p}=1.0 \times 10^{2}{/tex} at TK
If degree of association of 0.10 M acetic acid is 0.8 at TK, then the ratio of the dimer to monomer is:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {\mathop {2C{H_3}COOH}\limits_{0.1} }\limits_{0.1(1 - \alpha )} }\limits_{0.1 \times 0.2} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {\mathop {{{(C{H_3}COOH)}_2}}\limits_0 }\limits_{\frac{{0.1\alpha }}{2}} }\limits_{\frac{{0.1 \times 0.8}}{2}} {/tex}  {tex}(\because \alpha=0.8){/tex}
{tex}\therefore K_{c}=\frac{\left[\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}\right)_{2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}\right]^{2}}=1.0 \times 10^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{[\text { Dimer }]}{[\text { monomer }]}=\frac{\left[\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}\right)_{2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COOH}\right]}=\frac{0.1 \times 0.8}{2 \times 0.1 \times 0.2}{/tex} = 2

questions.question: Potash alum when dissolved in water, produces:
questions.explanation: Al2(SO4)3 = acidic salt so potash alum produce acidic solution.

questions.question:
Which of the following reaction(s) is/are homogeneous?

  1. 2NO(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g)
  2. C(s) + CO(2) + 2Cl2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2COCl2(g)
  3. HC2H3O2(aq) + H2O(l) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} {tex}{H}_{3} {O}_{({aq})}^{+}{/tex} + {tex}{C}_{2} {H}_{3}O_{2(aq)}^{-}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
1. 2NO(g) + O2(g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NO2(g)
  2. HC2H3O2(aq) + H2O(l) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} {tex}{H}_{3} {O}_{({aq})}^{+}{/tex} + {tex}{C}_{2} {H}_{3}O_{2(aq)}^{-}{/tex}

questions.question:
Consider the following homogeneous equilibrium involving gases.

For this reaction, Kp = 9.57 at 500 K.
Suppose 57.5 g of each species is initially placed in a 100 decilitre reaction vessel. Which of the following is TRUE?

options.option: The reaction will move in forward direction to attain equilibrium.
The reaction will move in backward direction to attain equilibrium.
The reaction remain constant to attain equilibrium.

questions.explanation:
= C2H6
 = C5H12
 = CH4
 = C6H14
[CH4] = 57.5g CH4 {tex}\times \frac{1 {mol} {CH}_{4}}{16 {g} {CH}_{4}}{/tex}
= 3.59 mol
= 3.59 mol in 100 decilitre i.e., 3.59 mol in 10 litre
{tex}\therefore{/tex} [CH4] = 0.067 M
Qc = {tex}\frac{\left[{CH}_{4}\right]\left[{C}_{6} {H}_{14}\right]}{\left[{C}_{2} {H}_{6}\right]\left[{C}_{5} {H}_{12}\right]}{/tex} = 1.57
Kp = Kc = 9.57
Qc < Kc
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The reaction will proceed in the forward direction.

questions.question:
4.0 moles of argon and 5.0 moles of PCl5 are introduced into an evacuated flask of 100 litre capacity at 610 K. The system is allowed to equilibrate. At equilibrium, the total pressure of mixture was found to be 6.0 atm. The Kp for the reaction is
[Given: R = 0.082 L atm K-1 mol-1]
questions.explanation:
PCl5 = 5 mole; Ar = 4 mole
PTotal = {tex}\frac{9 \times 0.82 \times 610}{100}{/tex} = 4.5 atm

{tex}\mathrm{X}_{\mathrm{PCl}_5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex}; XAO = {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{PCl}_5}{/tex} = {tex} \frac{5 \times 4.5}{9}{/tex} = 2.5 atm; PAr = {tex}\frac{4 \times 4.5}{9}{/tex} = 2 atm
{tex}\underset{2.5-P}{{\mathrm{PCl}}_5}\rightleftharpoons\underset P{{\mathrm{PCl}}_3}+\underset P{{\mathrm{Cl}}_2}{/tex}
Ptotal = 2.5 - P + P + P +PAr = 6 atm
P = 1.5 atm
KP = {tex}\frac{1.5 \times 1.5}{1}{/tex} = 2.25

questions.question: For the dissolution of solids in liquids, at a given temperature, the constant is ________.
questions.explanation: It is because at a particular temperature only a certain amount of solute can be dissolved. But it may be increased after increasing the temperature, so the solubility is constant at a given temperature.

questions.question:
In a closed system;
{tex}A(s)\:\rightleftharpoons2B(g)+3C(g){/tex}
If the partial pressure of C is doubled, then the partial pressure of B will be:
questions.explanation:
For the closed system A(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B(g)+3C(g)
Kp = ( PB )2(PC)3.......(1)
Thus, Kp is constant, if we double the partial pressure of C, P'C =2PC
Kp= (P'B)2(P'C)3 = (P'B)2(2PC)3 = 8(P'B)2(PC)3 .....(2)
From (1) and( 2), we get
8( P'B)2 = (PB)2
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {{{{P}'}}_{B}} \right)=\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\left( {{P}_{B}} \right){/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(s){/tex} + 2H2O(v) Kp = 1086 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 atm2 at 25oC. The efflorescent nature of {tex}\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}{/tex} can be noticed when vapour pressure of H2O in atmosphere is:
questions.explanation:
An efflorescent salt is one that loses H2O to the atmosphere.
For the reaction,
{tex}\mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(s) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CuSO}_{4} \cdot 3 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}(s){/tex} + 2H2O(v)
{tex}K_{p}=\left(P_{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}}^{\prime}\right)^{2}=1.086 \times 10^{-4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ P_{\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}}^{\prime}=1.042 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~atm}{/tex} = 7.92 mm
{tex}\because{/tex} If {tex}P_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}^{\prime}{/tex} at 25oC, < 7.92 mm only then, the reaction will proceed in forward direction.

questions.question:
A 20 litre container at 400 K contains CO2 (g) at pressure 0.4 atm and an excess of SrO neglect the volume of solid (SrO). The volume of the container is now decreased by moving the movable piston fitted in the container. The maximum volume of the container, when the pressure of CO2 attains its maximum value,
(Given that: SrCO3 (s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} SrO(s) + CO2 (g) Kp = 1.6 atm)
questions.explanation:
SrCO3(s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} SrO(s) + CO2(g); Kp = {tex}P_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}={/tex} 1.6 atm
The reaction should favour forward direction as Q < Kp, but since SrCO3 is not taken initially, the reaction cannot take place in forward direction.
SrO(s) + CO2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} SrCO3; Kp = {tex}\frac{1}{1.6}{/tex} = 0.625 atm
Thus on increasing pressure, volume of container will decrease till {tex}P_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}{/tex} becomes 1.6 atm having the same mole of CO2.
The reaction will attain equilibrium till P = 0.625
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Initial mole of CO2 {tex}=\frac{P V}{R T}=\frac{0.4 \times 20}{R T}=\frac{8}{R T}{/tex}
At eq. V {tex}=\frac{n R T}{P}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{R T} \times \frac{R T}{1.6}{/tex} = 5 litre

questions.question:
Two solids A and B shows the following equilibria in a vessel:
A(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} X(g) + 2Y (g); {tex}K_{p_1}{/tex} = 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3
A(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Z + 2Y (g); {tex}K_{p_2}{/tex} = 4.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3
What will be the total pressure over a mixture of A and B in atm?

At a certain temperature, KP for dissociation of solid CaCO3 is 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 atm and for the reaction, C(s) + CO2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2CO is 2.0 atm respectively. Calculate the pressure of CO at this temperature when solid C, CaO, CaCO3 are mixed and allowed to attain equilibrium.
questions.explanation:
C(s) + CO2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2CO
Kp = {tex}\frac{\left(P_{\mathrm{CO}}\right)^{2}}{{P}_{\mathrm{CO}_{2}}^{\prime}}{/tex} = 2.0
Now both are at ewuilibrium and thus {tex}P_{CO_2}{/tex} = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 which should remain constant, thus
2.0 = {tex}\frac{\left(P_{C O}^{\prime}\right)^{2}}{4 \ \times \ 10^{-2}}{/tex}
{tex}P_{C O}^{\prime}=\sqrt{4 \times 10^{-2} \times 2}{/tex} = 0.28 atm

questions.question:
Would 1% CO2 in air be sufficient to prevent any loss in weight when M2CO3 is heated at 120°C?
M2CO3(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} M2O(s) + CO2(g)
KP = 0.0095 atm at 120°C. How long would the partial pressure of CO2 have to be to promote this reaction at 120°C?
questions.explanation: Yes. 0.0095 atm

questions.question: 20 litres container at 400 K contain CO2(g) at pressure 0.4 atm and an excess of SrO (neglect the volume of solid SrO). The volume of the container is now decreased by moving the movable piston fitted in the container. The maximum volume of the container, when the pressure of CO2 attains its maximum value, will be ________. (Given that: SrCO3(S) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} SrO(s) + CO2(g), Kp = 1.6 atm)
questions.explanation:
SrCO3(s) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} SrO(s) + CO2(g)
Kp = {tex}{P}_{{CO}_{2}}{/tex} = 1.6 atm
P1 = 0.4 atm, V1 = 20 L, T1 = 400 K
P2 = Kp = 1.6 atm, V2 = ?, T2 = 400 K
{tex}\frac{P_{1} V_{1}}{T_{1}}=\frac{P_{2} V_{2}}{T_{2}}{/tex}
Since T1 = T2,
V2 = {tex}\frac{{P}_{1} {~V}_{1}}{{P}_{2}}=\frac{0.4 \times 20}{1.6}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} V2 = 5L

questions.question: The dynamic nature of chemical equilibrium can be demonstrated in the synthesis of ammonia by Haber's process. Choose the appropriate option given below:

options.option: by maintaining high temperature and low pressure

questions.explanation: The use of isotope (deuterium) in the formation of ammonia clearly indicates that chemical reactions reach a state of dynamic equilibrium in which the rates of forward and reverse reactions are equal and there is no net change in composition.

questions.question: If the Kc is neither too small nor too large, one of the following will happen:

options.option: both reactants and products are at equilibrium

questions.explanation:
A High value of K is a high concentration of products. If Kc > 103, products predominate over reactants.

  • If Kc < 10–3, reactants predominate over products, i.e., if Kc is very small, the reaction proceeds rarely.
  • If Kc is in the range of 10– 3 to 103, appreciable concentrations of both reactants and products are present.

questions.question: A particular ratio of product to reactant helps in predicting the direction in which a given reaction will proceed at any stage is called ________.
questions.explanation:
The reaction quotient Q. For a general reaction: aA + bB ⇌ cC + dD
Qc = [C]c [D]d / [A]a [B]b
Then, If Qc > Kc,
the reaction will proceed in the direction of reactants (reverse reaction).
If Qc < Kc, the reaction will proceed in the direction of the products (forward reaction).
If Qc = Kc, the reaction mixture is already at equilibrium.

questions.question: In the lime kiln, the reversible reaction, CaCO3 (s) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} CaO3(s) + CO2 (g) proceeds to completion because:
questions.explanation: Removal of the product always favours forward reaction.

questions.question: A chemical reaction A {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} B is said to be at equilibrium when:

options.option: the rate of transformation of A to B is just equal to the rate of transformation of Bio A in the system
complete conversion of A to B has taken place
only 10% conversion of A to B has taken place
conversion of A to B is only 50% complete

questions.explanation: Chemical equilibrium is a state when the concentration of reactants and products do not change with time. It is attained when the rate of forwarding reaction becomes equal to the rate of backward reaction.

questions.question:
An aqueous solution of hydrogen sulphide shows the equilibrium,
H2S {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} H+ + HS-
If dilute hydrochloric acid is added to an aqueous solution of hydrogen sulphide without any change in temperature, then:

options.option: The concentration of HS^- will decrease
The concentration of HS^- will increase
The concentration of undissociated hydrogen sulphide will decrease

questions.explanation:
Ka for H2S {tex}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{HS}^{-}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right]}{/tex}
An increase in [H+] will show a decrease in [HS-] to maintain constant Ka value.

questions.question: Hydrogen and oxygen were heated together in a closed vessel. The equilibrium constant is found to decrease after 2000°C. Which is responsible for this?

options.option: Both forward and backward reaction have same rate
It is a property of the system, hence no reason for lower value

questions.explanation: It is a property of the system, hence no reason for a lower value.

questions.question: If 1.0 mole of I2 is introduced into 1.0 litre flask at 1000 K, at equilibrium (Kc = 10-6), which one is correct?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {{I_2}}\limits_{1 - x} \rightleftharpoons \mathop {2{I^ - }}\limits_{2x} ;{/tex}
Find x, using Kc {tex}=10^{-6}=\frac{4 x^{2}}{(1-x)}{/tex}, Find x and notice 2x < (1 - x).

questions.question: What weight of solid ammonium carbamate (NH2COONH4) when vaporised at 200°C will have a volume of 8.96 litre at 1.0 atm pressure. Assume that the solid completely decomposes into CO2 and NH3 at 200°C and 1.0 atm?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\mathop {\mathop {}\limits_{Before\;dissociation} }\limits_{After\;dissociation} \mathop {\mathop {N{H_2}COON{H_4}}\limits_a }\limits_0 \to \mathop {\mathop {2N{H_3}}\limits_0 }\limits_{2a} + \mathop {\mathop {C{O_2}}\limits_0 }\limits_a {/tex}
Total mole after decomposition = 3a
Thus PV = nRT
{tex}\therefore \ 1 \times 8.96=3 a \times 0.0821 \times 473{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a = 0.077
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Mass of NH2COONH4 = 0.077 {tex}\times{/tex} 78 = 6.0 g

questions.question: The equilibrium constant Kc for A(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} B(g) is 1.1, gas B will have molar concentration greater than 1 if:
questions.explanation: Kc = {tex}\frac{[B]}{[A]}{/tex} = 1.1

questions.question: The value of log10Ko for a reaction is A {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} B: (Given {tex}\Delta_{r} \mathrm{H}_{298}{/tex} = -54.07 kJ mol-1, {tex}\Delta_{r} \mathrm{S}_{298 \mathrm{~K}}^{0}=10 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}{/tex} and R = 8.314 JK-1 mol-1, 2.303 {tex}\times{/tex} 8.314 {tex}\times{/tex} 298 = 5705):
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Delta G^{\circ}=\Delta H^{\circ}-T \Delta S^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}=-54.07 \times 10^{3}-298 \times 10{/tex} = -57050
Now {tex}-\Delta G^{\circ}=2.303 \mathrm{RT} \log K^{\circ}{/tex}
57050 {tex}=2.303 \times 8.314 \times 298 \log K^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ \log K^{\circ}=\frac{57050}{2.303 \times 8.314 \times 298}=\frac{57050}{5705}{/tex} = 10

questions.question: Pure ammonia is placed in a vessel at a temperature where its dissociation constant (Kp) is appreciable. At equilibrium:

options.option: concentration of hydrogen is less than that of nitrogen
K_p does not change significantly with pressure
concentration of NH_3 does not change with pressure

questions.explanation: Kp depends on temperature

questions.question: The equilibrium constants {tex}K_{P_{1}}{/tex} and {tex}K_{P_{2}}{/tex} for the reactions X {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2Y and Z {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} P + Q, respectively are in the ratio of 1 : 9. If the degree of dissociation of X and Z be equal then the ratio of total pressure at these equilibria is:
questions.explanation:
Given: {tex}\frac{K_{P_{1}}}{K_{P_{2}}}=\frac{1}{9}{/tex} and {tex}\alpha_{x}=\alpha_{z}{/tex}
{tex}\mathop {\mathop X\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - \alpha } \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {2Y}\limits_0 }\limits_{2\alpha } {/tex} {tex}\therefore K_{P_{1}}=\frac{\left(2 \alpha_{x}\right)^{2}}{\left(1-\alpha_{x}\right)} \times\left[\frac{P_{1}}{1+\alpha_{x}}\right]^{1}{/tex}
{tex}\mathop {\mathop Z\limits_1 }\limits_{1 - \alpha } \rightleftharpoons \mathop {\mathop {P + Q}\limits_0 }\limits_{2\alpha } {/tex} {tex}\therefore K_{P_{2}}=\frac{\left(\alpha_{z}\right)^{2}}{\left(1-\alpha_{z}\right)} \times\left[\frac{P_{2}}{1+\alpha_{z}}\right]^{1}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{P_{1}}{P_{2}}=\frac{K_{P_{1}}}{K_{P_{2}}} \times \frac{1}{4}=\frac{1}{9} \times \frac{1}{4}=\frac{1}{36}{/tex}

questions.question: In which of the following cases, does the reaction go farthest to completion?
questions.explanation: Higher is the value of K, more is the probability for a reaction to go for completion.

questions.question:
If K1 and K2 are the respective equilibrium constants for the two reactions,
XeF6(g) + H2O(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} XeOF4(g) + 2HF(g)
XeO4(g) + XeF6(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} XeOF4(g) + XeO3F2(g)
the equilibrium constant of the reaction,
XeO4(g) + 2HF6(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} XeO3F2(g) + H2O(g) will be:
If the value of an equilibrium constant for a particular reaction is 1.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 1012, then at equilibrium the system will contain:
questions.explanation: The value of K(K = 1.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 1012) is very high so the system will proceed with forward reaction and thus will contain mostly products at equilibrium.

questions.question: If the equilibrium constant for N2(g) + O2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO(g) is K, the equilibrium constant for {tex}\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NO}(g){/tex} will be:
questions.explanation:
For N2(g) + O2(g) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} 2NO; {tex}K=\frac{[\mathrm{NO}]^{2}}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]}{/tex}
For {tex}\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NO} ; K^{\prime}=\frac{\mathrm{NO}}{\left[\mathrm{N}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}\left[\mathrm{O}_{2}\right]^{1 / 2}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore K^{\prime}=\sqrt{K}{/tex}

questions.question:

Let a mol of each PCl3 and Cl2 be taken to attain equilibrium

[{tex}\because{/tex} 40% of 8 mole reacts i.e., 3.2 mole reacts in the ratio of 1 : 3 i.e., 0.8 mole of N2 and 2.4 mole of H2 reacts
Total mole = 6.4
V {tex}\propto{/tex} mole at constant T
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{V_{i}}{V_{f}}=\frac{8}{6.4}=\frac{10}{8}=\frac{5}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: The ionisation constant of {tex}\mathrm{NH}_{4}^{+}{/tex} in water is 5.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-10 at 25oC. questions.explanation:
{tex}NH_3+H_2O\;\overset{k_f}{\underset{k_b}\rightleftharpoons}\;NH_4^++OH^-{/tex}; Kb = 3.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1010
{tex}\mathrm{NH}_{4}^{+}{/tex} + H2O {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} NH4OH + H+; Ka = 5.6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-10
{tex}K_{\text {baseNH }_{3}}=\frac{K_{f}}{K_{b}}{/tex}
Also, {tex}K_{\text {baseNH }_{3}}=\frac{K_{w}}{K_{\text {acid } \mathrm{NH}_{4}^{+}}}{/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} Kacid {tex}\times{/tex} Kbase = Kw)

questions.question:
The % yield of ammonia as a function of time in the reaction
{tex}\mathrm{N}_{2}(g)+3 \mathrm{H}_{2}(g) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3}(g), \Delta H<0{/tex}
at (P, T1) is given below:

If this reaction is conducted at (P, T2), with T2 > T1, the % yield of ammonia as a function of time is represented by:
questions.explanation: The combination of N2 and H2 to give NH3 takes place at slower rate if temperature is reduced to 473 K (200°C) because fewer energetic collisions occurs between molecules, however yield of NH3 is 88%. On the other hand, the combination occurs at faster rate at 723 K (450°C) but the yield of NH3 is 15%. Actually it is the number of energetic collisions which really matters to lead a chemical reaction and the rate of reaction is reduced at low temperature (473 K). The 15% yield of NH3 is better at 723 K because equilibrium is achieved earlier. The NH3 formed is dissolved in water and is drained off as NH3(aq) from the system so that unreacted N2 and H2 recombines again to produce NH3 in order to maintain constant value of Kc. Thus, all the N2 and H2 are converted to NH3 with a faster rate.

questions.question: The equilibrium mixture of reaction : 2NH3 (g) {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} N2 + 3H2 diffuses twice as fast as SO2 under similar conditions. What is % dissociation of NH3 at equilibrium?
questions.explanation:
2NH3( g) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} N2 + 3H2
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{SO}_{2}}}{\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{mix}}}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{M}_{\operatorname{mix}}}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{SO}_{2}}}}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
Or rate of diffusion {tex}\propto{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{M}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{M}_{\operatorname{mix}}=\frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{SO}_{2}}}{4}=\frac{32+32}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Mmix = 16
Let ′x′ fraction NH3 converts to N2 and H2′
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex}Mmix = 2(1 - x) {tex}\times{/tex} 17 + 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 28 {tex}\times{/tex} x
{tex}16=\frac{34-34 x+34 x}{2(1-x)+3 x+x}{/tex}
{tex}16=\frac{34}{2+2 x}{/tex}
32 + 32x = 34
32x = 2
{tex}X=\frac{2}{32}=\frac{1}{16}{/tex}
% convert to = {tex}\frac{100}{16}{/tex} = 6.25%

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. In equilibrium mixture of ice and water kept in perfectly insulated flask, mass of ice and water does not change with time.
  2. The intensity of red colour increases when oxalic acid is added to a solution containing iron (III) nitrate and potassium thiocyanate.
  3. On addition of catalyst, the equilibrium constant value is not affected.
  4. Equilibrium constant for a reaction with negative {tex}\Delta{/tex}H value decreases as the temperature increases.
questions.explanation:
Iron (III) nitrate and potassium thiocyanate react to give deep red coloration. The reaction is:
{tex}\underset {\text{Yellow}}{Fe_{aq}^{3+}}+SCN^− \underset{\text{deep red }}{↔[FeSCN]_{aq}^{2+}}{/tex}
Now, to this solution, if oxalic acid (H2C2O4) is added, then it reacts with Fe3+ ions to form the stable complex ion [Fe(C2O4)3]3-, thus decreasing the concentration of Fe3+ ions. In accordance with the Le-Chatelier's principle, the decrease in the concentration of Fe3+ ions, leads to the decrease in the concentration of [FeSCN]2+ ions which is indicated by the fact that the colour of the solution becomes lighter.

questions.question: N2O4 is dissociated to 33% and 40% at total pressure P1 and P2 atm respectively. Then the ratio {tex}\frac {P_1}{P_2}{/tex} is:

{tex}\Sigma{/tex}n = 1 - {tex}\alpha{/tex} + 2{tex}\alpha{/tex}
= 1 + {tex}\alpha{/tex}
Kp = {tex}\frac{{P}_{\left[\mathrm{NO}_{2}\right]^{2}}}{{P}_{\left[{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}\right]}}{/tex}
Kp = {tex}\frac{\left({n}_{\mathrm{N} \mathrm{O}_{2}}\right)^{2}}{{n}_{\mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}}} \times\left[\frac{{P}}{\sum {n}}\right]^{1}{/tex}
For 33% dissociation: Kp = {tex}\frac{(2\ \times \ 0.33)^{2}}{0.67} \times\left[\frac{{P}_{1}}{{1 . 3 3}}\right]{/tex}
For 40% dissociation: Kp = {tex}\frac{(2\ \times \ 0.40)^{2}}{0.60} \times\left[\frac{{P}_{2}}{{1 .40}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{P}_{1}}{{P}_{2}}=1.56 \simeq 1.60=\frac{8}{5}{/tex}

2Ag+ + Cu {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} Cu2+ + 2Ag
At equilibrium ax
if V is 500mL

to have Kc constant [Cu2+] will be less than {tex}\frac x{2}{/tex}

Equimolar mixture of N2 and O2 is allowed to attain equilibrium. The fraction of N2 and O2 converted into NO is:

questions.question: For the equilibrium, N2 + 3H2 {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2NH3, Kc at 1000 K is 2.37 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-3. If at equilibrium [N2] = 2M, [H2] = 3M, the concentration of NH3 is ________.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 0.358 M

questions.question: The elevation of lake A is 4350 m, lake B is 5486 m and lake C is 2150 m. If water temperatures are the same, which lake has a lower concentration of oxygen gas?

A {tex}\rightleftharpoons{/tex} 2B

(M) A B
I 0.5 0
